top_rant.text:
PROS: IT SEARS BEEF EXCELLENT! high temps make grilling EEZYBREEZY! small size PERFECT for our small patio
CONS: ok-i hate cleaning it-but i hate cleaning EVERYTHING! wish they would have added the side burner even with this grills smaller sized grill

MY LONG REVIEW:

ok--i used to HATE grilling.

whenever we had cookouts at our place, i found the man to MAN our grill because i never could get the knack of it. with the heat fluctuating all the time, i found it stressful to grill and oftentimes would just SWOON over other's success at it---i wanted to be like THEM!!!! but no, it always happened the same way-- sometimes things turned out--sometimes they didnt (& usually DIDNT when my in-laws were over for supper! haha)--SO--plain and simple--i hated it.

with a PASSION!!

have i mentioned how much i DID NOT like grilling!!!???? hahaha!!!

since recently relocating to florida, i was NOT looking forward to getting another grill. i felt a terrible pit in my stomach just thinking about how much money we were gonna waste when we bought another grill that i was gonna watch deteriorate through my kitchen window from lack of use.

spring arrives and there we are at a favorite place of ours: LOWES.

yippeee!!! will ya just LOOK at all those shiny NEW grills!!! hubby happily drags me through the rows and rows of them and we start reading, investigating, comparing, learning.

i HAD to pay attention because by the look on my wonderful husbands face--

we were going home with a grill that day--NO DOUBT ABOUT IT.

which i thought was kind of silly because i could SEE him thinking about those grilled steaks THAT I COULD NEVER MAKE CORRECTLY--which also means EDIBLE!!!! how could he have forgotten how much i DIDNT grill before???

whatever--we were getting one--so i figured i better hunker down and really look at them.

first thing i noticed was --there are some seriously MONSTER grills out there!!!

even when we came upon the Char Broil Quantum Infrared Series, i kept thinking "too much too much!!!"

too big--i didnt want all that space! i dont even KNOW anyone in florida that we can invite over to FILL all that space up with food! and theres only me-darling hubby-and 17 yr old son!

but this Char Broil line really struck me as user friendly.

it made me imagine that even i could grill successfully.

thats when the magic happened for me.

all the way down at the end of the monsters was a very small version off the monsters.

and i mean small! rhode island could kick the crap out of it in a fight-- THATS how small i'm talking!

;-)

the side shelves folded down & created a patio version size that didnt intimidate me.

i read about all the infrared features and was getting just a little bit excited about it.

could i actually make a steak that wasn't "jerkey"? the more i pondered it, the more my husband kept commenting on how cute it was! (he doesn't use the word "cute" that often, so this was momentous to say the least) but he was right!!! it IS cute.

just enough for us.

so . . .

thats it!

we bought it. picked out a nice Char Broil cover, bought a propane tank with fuel, and were on our way home to put it together.

darling husband (dh) had that thing put together in about 1-1 1/2 hours.

he took his time and did it in FRONT of the tv!!!!

aargh!

so i had to WAIT until i could actually get it outside to season it!

i read the directions (because i'm a GIRL--THATS why!!),

then proceeded to BATHE & wipe it completely down with peanut oil.

went online to see if i couldnt find some reviews to read (because it's AFTER you buy the product that its good to know what you paid for, of course. . .i mean, we're not STUPID ya know! ahem.)
while the grill was burning off the oil--thus seasoning itself.

oh boy.

i hardly found ONE SINGLE POSITIVE review!!!!!

--complaints GALORE about it parts of the grill body rusting, the gratees were rusting, the infrared pans were clogging, yadda yadda yadda bad grill bad grill!!!

"honey? heh heh . . . these reviews dont look so good"

yeah, i got a bit nervous--but it was good that i read those reviews.

next day i bought steaks.

charbroil.com had some HANDY videos on tips & techniques and recipes and i watched that guy grill his strip steaks to perfection and went immediately to the commissary to buy OUR steaks.

ohhh i could hardly contain myself i was sooo excited.

went online again to get a great Steak Butter recipe that i could apply to the steaks to melt on them afterward while they were "resting".

guess what???

these

steaks

ROCKED!!!!!!

i am not kidding! i grilled them to perfection!!

my husband literally MOANED while he ate his and kept naming off all of our favorite steak houses that MY steaks were better than!!! hahaha!!!

i felt RIDICULOUSLY triumphant!!

im serious--honeys--you'd think i WORKED for Charb Broil!!! i was and AM STILL sooooo STOKED about this grill (no pun intended).

ive been using it nearly EVERY week since purchasing it ! !

i am getting a feel for the high temps and what they do with different types meats--and different thicknesses of meat-- and i am in hog heaven now.

seriously.

you heard it here first people---

i LOVELOVELOVE GRILLINNNNG!!!!!!!!!!

this infrared grill and the pans under them make everything sooo easy without the flare-ups that usually ruined my meals. i follow the directions for cleaning it and seasoning it everytime i use it--i dont LIKE TO CLEAN IT--but i DO IT--it doesnt take but about 5 minutes!!

those folks that have complained about what the grates "LOOKED" like--where its "rusty looking" it's actually SEASONING!!

they are SUPPOSED to be UGLY UGLY UGLY!!!

you arent supposed to clean all that black seasoning OFF of them---they arent supposed to STAY looking nice and silver!!!

so what that here and there where i havent "oiled" them there is a tad bit of surface-y rust--nothing serious--it's just the same as when you use an iron pot---ya gotta keep it oiled so it seasons properly!

it rained sideways here with a bad storm one night and water got up under our grill cover--so right on the floor where the door closes on the inside theres a spot of surface rust.

if having a perfectly beautiful looking grill is what matters to you---then this probably isnt the grill for you.

i, myself, dont care that the black paint is peeling on the inside of my grill because of the 500 degree heat!!! lol

these are things that do NOT matter to me when folks are sitting around myyyy table all moaning and humming about how good their food is!!

so--i know this is the longest review you've ever read--its actually only the second and longest one ive ever written--so im a bit on the yappy side, i KNOW!!!!

but i am one happy little puppy here on the panhandle of florida enjoying the snot outta this GREAT GRILL!!!

i needed to post this here on amazon's reviews, because i thought that maybe i could write a realistic & needed "common sense" ? review about this grill---it certainly isn't PERFECT for everyone.

but its perfect for me, and for someone that literally felt like she had the FLU whenever the subject of grilling a meal came up--- i'd say that says a lot.

i love it. so much, i would marry it.

its true!!

i actually wanna marry my grill.

heh heh heh.
top_rant.title: Oh how i love lthee,  let me count the ways!

top_rant.text:
Opening statement: An engineer friend(Mechanical/electrical/electronic - a very knowledgeable fellow.) tells me that "...due to the very high water pressures involved - enough to cause injury if not careful - plus the 120 V A/C "right there" with the water - enough to cause death, if not careful, along with the restrictive budgets of trying to make something that, generally costs 1000's, for a 100 or 3, means that the darned thing(For safety reasons and for just plain "it's cheap" reasons.), breaks at the drop of a hat, at the slightest provocation. Shuts down, permanently and is "done for", with no repair possible..." SURE! It's "possible" to fix the widget, but not by you and me and not for a price any professional would have to charge.

Preamble: Why do you buy a cheap pressure washer? It's cheap! Most of us don't need a "pro" anything. It's a throwaway item. Buy it, use it, send it in for your "refurb" when it breaks, for as long as the warranty lasts. After the warranty, when it breaks, throw it away and go get a new one. How do you make it "pay"? I just called my local rental place. $50 a day, for a gas-powered model(I've seen them, they're "nice" gas models, but nothing "SUPER"! Probably cost 12-1500 dollars.). What makes the rental ones, or professional ones "professional"? They are repairable. Meaning that they cost enough to make it worthwhile TO have them repaired - period. One generally pays a HUGE price, these days, for ANYTHING repairable - period. TV's, cell phones nearly anything. Most of us don't need our pressure washers everyday, or even every 4 months, so we don't need to spend 1000's on it. All we need to do is make it "pay-off" the rental fee we would otherwise have paid, before it runs out of warranty. Tah-Dah! Use your pressure washer 5 "days", in the first year, and you have "used-up" 300+ dollars, when you include running to get the thing at the rental place and taking it back(IF your entire town didn't pick the same weekend to DO THEIR PW-ing, and they even have one TO rent..? So, pay the same as, or less than the number of days you'll use it in a year, and when it breaks the second season(?), "be happy" and go get another one - period. If it MUST last a long time, pay the big money, at a "real", professional, pressure washer dealer, for a real pressure washer and - when it breaks, anyway, be prepared to "take it back" for service. Out of warranty, a minor repair may cost you, in total, more than the cheap electric PW - period. That's business. For your home DIY needs..?

On with the show:

1. Buy it at a place that offers "no problem" returns/exchanges. Check the wording of their guarantee! Ask PRIOR to purchase, what will happen "if"? Make sure you get a reasonable period(Couple weeks?) to be sure it works OK. It's cheap, it was made "somewhere" where folks work for very little money, so you must realize that it ain't always gonna' be "perfect" the first, second or even third time(Come to think of it --- that can happen no matter where it's made, no matter how much you spend!). In any event, THE SELLER is responsible for the first(Short?) amount of time for you to "take it back/send it back" - the manufacturer is(Generally speaking) not. Your new car doesn't go back to the factory for replacement either if it doesn't work "out of the box" - you handle it through your(Reliable? Knowledgeable? Fair? Properly compensated???) dealer...

2. Take the thing home. Read the manual. Better yet??? READ THE MANUAL BEFORE YOU BUY THE STUPID THING!!! Every manual for every thing is online! DO NOT go to the store, pick whatever looks good, or whatever's cheapest, and expect miracles. Try the thing. Try all the parts. If anything "came broken", take it ALL back for an exchange. If it seems "OK"(No obviously busted parts.), but you can't get it to work - read the manual again. Ask your neighbor who you know has one(Good opportunity to actually meet them!). Go on the web and look for "hints". If all else fails - Take It Back.

3. How to make the thing work. READ THE MANUAL. Did I mention - READ THE MANUAL? I would say that again, but, alas, the last manual I had for a cheap, electric Karcher Pressure Washer, was not all that informative - so much for the manual. Read it anyway, but, don't expect to know everything when you're done. I've found alot of excellent(And many, many v-e-r-y stupid!) "hints" on the web. Good luck with that, but the web can be a good source of information, if you're careful.

4. May be a good idea to buy a "connector", at the hardware store, to allow you to get the water source hose on/off easier. The hardware person will probably know just what you need - and if not, find another hardware person. Still, it is NOT necessary. Take your time attaching and removing the hose, thread it on there carefully(Best when the thing
is dry. If not dry, try "sticky/thin" gloves to get a better grip on everything.). EVERYTHING IS PLASTIC! Don't throw it around - frankly, the darned thing is "delicate" - not made to absorb your "moods". If you do buy the fancy connector, don't forget to remove it prior to returning or throwing the busted PW away! The "better" the hose, itself, and IT'S connectors, the easier time you'll have. If you have a short(Few feet?) piece of hose, this will work, too, to make it easier to get the long hose off/on. Just leave your "feed" hose ON the unit. DO NOT "stress" anything! Be nice to it and it will treat you v-e-r-y well in return.

5. How to make the soap work: If this is in the manual, I missed it - several times(Or my manual for my units didn't have the info, at the time.). Think of the local DIY car wash. The "low pressure sudsing(Sudzing? Soaping.)" mode. With the "generic" spray attachment on it, set it to "MIX" - the lowest pressure setting. NOW it soaps!!! And it runs through the soap like it, too, is water - FAST! VERY FAST! Run the thing over whatever you want to soap as fast as you can, then turn the unit back to high pressure. If you're smart enough to do your PW'ing in the shade, good for you(I'm not.), you might allow the soap to "sit" a minute or two(Don't let it dry!), before hitting it with the high pressure "rinse". Or, soap it, turn off the unit, get out your sponge or brush and do some work, yourself, for gosh sake! I am using "Krud Cutter" - seems to help.

5a. My unit came with a brush! When I attach it, it is, apparently "always" low-pressure... So I can brush the worst of the gunk off the whatever - just like at the carwash(How handy! Only took me a few years to figure that one out.)...

6. The "buzzer". That's what I call it - I think it's called the "Vario Wand"(?). WHAT A GADGET!!! Plowed-off hard, baked-on matted, grass under my mower deck like a chisel and sledge hammer! Covered me with the gook, too, but, that's part of the fun, ey? Makes it look like you really were working! This thing will take paint off! At least loose paint. A good/bad thing. Be careful where you must be. Though, if it takes the paint off, whatever it is needs repainting - right???

6b. What does a "real" pressure washer get you? Maybe more pressure? Frankly, more pressure than these little units can provide is completely unnecessary for most jobs(In my "home-use" opinion.). What a professional unit can do is make the job faster, by providing more actual water, or - providing Pressure Washing over a larger area(Say a 6 inch swath as opposed to an inch or two?). Again, if it's your business, you need that. And, if you actually do want to wash your car every week - or more often, these little things just aren't for you - too much setup/teardown, if nothing else. For gettin' it out, hooking it up, and cleaning up your stuff once or twice a year, these are great. Downright fun - a blast!(Pun intended.). Take your time, hook things up properly. Tear it down completely. Store it properly. You could get a couple seasons out of the thing..?

Addendum: After Power Washing - particularly after "soaping"/Power Rinsing. Take the hose off the unit(Or use another hose), with no "end" on it, to "flood" the cleaned area for a really good rinse(Say... after PW'ing, but, before sealing your driveway.). Uses LOTS more water! Does a MUCH better rinse, where needed. Car washing pros have long recommended such a "low pressure/flood" rinse(I even have a special hose nozzle for it.).

7. BEWARE!!! There is a FILTER inside(Down in) the inlet/water source/garden hose connector(This is in the manual!). Having a "second" filter, in any "optional" connector you might buy should be a good thing, if available. Otherwise(And even so), pull it out, carefully, once in awhile - and(Gently) clean it.

8. How much water pressure do you need to supply it? None. That's right - NONE! The Karcher(At least mine??? Though it doesn't say it in either manual - one of those helpful web hints.), can "pull" it's own water from a bucket or barrel or stream, all by itself. Cool! That said, if you have a small(2000 watt?) generator, and some source of water, you could go almost anywhere to clean stuff! It only takes a couple of gallons of water, per minute, so 5, 10, 20 gallons in the back of your lawn cart "X" minutes of use is quite do-able - and you actually can clean pretty quick with some practice. I feed it at my cabin(Electric but no nearby "standard" water source to everything I want to clean.) by running one of those little 1/3(?) HP "utility/submersible" pumps in my little stream, and a length of garden hose(As the stream is a good 5 feet below where the PW is - qwuite a "draw".). But, I could just as soon throw the hose-end IN the stream and try it, to see if it worked. If it does, you're good! That said, using an "alternate" water source, like a stream or pond, one should be MUCH more careful with the filtering! You'll have to figure it out for your situation. You do NOT want to get dirt in the thing, as that may break it in seconds. It's own filter should be "OK", if kept clean. All said - I use a 3/4 inch hose(Usual is 5/8) to give it the best "shot"... Our Water Pressure is about 70 PSI. Strictly speaking, that's overkill.

8a. If you "seem" to be having "feed water pressure problems", with the thing spitting/sputtering/not "running" or not "running right", it may be "AIR IN THE FEED HOSE"! Get the little "ends" for your hose which have the little on/off valves(Every hose end needs one). BEFORE you attach your hose to your PW, open the little valve on your hose, run the water, full-speed, until it runs with no air burps. turn the water off AT THE LITTLE VALVE, at the end of the hose you will now attach TO the PW! If you attach it without "filling-it-up"(Tech Term: Purging?), first, the hose may have air in it. A SPECIAL PROBLEM WITH MY little pump and pulling water from the stream. Everytime I disconnect, the water drains out clear back to the stream. Get it? The unit pumps water, fine. Air, not so much.

9. Try not to go NUTS with the widget!!! One thing that may "save" the thing for longer than the waranty period is to adopt a "policy". Say --- run it, pretty much continuously, for 20 or 30 minutes(However long you're willing to risk system overheating catastrophe?). Then, let it sit(Turn it off, but you can leave everything attached.), for a couple of hours. Let it cool down - it'll take awhile. Then do some more. Make a day of it - or a weekend - or a week! You do not have to return it before close of business(A good thing.). Me? I tend to turn the thing on, start cleaning, and clean everything in sight! If I don't control myself HOURS can go by, with the poor little thing cranking away, by then on my heighbors siding two houses down the street! It just isn't made for such treatment. On the other hand, if I got, say, 6, nearly continuous, hours out of it, in one day(Or any number of days in one year.).....? Then, it didn't work at all or forever-more the next time I turned it on.....? It would have paid for itself, wouldn't it? Easy!!! Compared to the rental deal............ And I would be perfectly happy going down to the store and picking up a new one(At least I'd try to be...).

This is the "Buy-it-cheap-and-throw-it-away-when-it-breaks society, folks. We need to learn how to live, today. We have no other time in which to live...... Make sure it works when you get it - if not, take it back to the dealer - NOW! Use it as much as you have to to make it "viable" before the warranty period ends. Take advantage of the warranty. Get any and all "refurbs" ASAP. Karcher DID offer 24 hour TS, years ago - when they first introduced cheap PW's, having no idea(Apparently?) of the astounding response they would get to their "little cheapy home cleaning machine". To keep it would have A. Put them out of business. B. Would have cost you 3, 4, 5x the money for your
"cheap" machine! Karcher STILL offers SUPER tech support! See your local, professional, Karcher Dealer, and buy a Pro PW. Not the "mart" nearest you, where low price is the only thing. You demand the lowest price for everything you buy? You get no "fancy" TS(Or fries) "with that"...

When they work, for as long as they work, they are amazing little machines, that do things previously not do-able by mere mortals. My last one cost less that it costs to fill my pickup truck, twice(About $120 per gas station visit - and I pump it myself and clean my own windshiled - NO TS at all!).

I guess enough time has gone by that I can go out and start on the back of the house(2 hours?). If it still works? It's in it's 3rd. season... ya' never know?). So far have done the driveway(20x40+) - twice - with soap and without. The camper - twice - with soap(And the brush) and without - and the driveway, and a big rock I dug-up last night(Actually, my spouse was fooling in the garden and "found it"... "I can't get this rock out? It's in the way") in a front garden bed - BIG ROCK! Neighbor took pity on me and came over to help. Dug around it, but couldn't get it out of the ground. Will take several people to roll it out onto the surface. However, it cleaned-up real pretty! So did the other rock(Long "out"), down by the street. Actually broke small pieces off the stone with the Vario(!), but took years of hardened "stuff" off it in seconds. Amazing. Also cleaned the mower(Mentioned elsewhere). Too much but, it's a nice, warm day and I just can't stop - what a gadget! What A Machine! Worth almost any amount of trouble - for the price. If I had the money I'd have a better one - maybe? But it would be bigger, gas-powered, louder, harder to store...... nah! This is just fine. Pain in the Yang, sometimes, but - fine. When it breaks? I'll be down picking up a new one, again.

I do NOT work for Karcher! Or anyone else, at the moment. I just had to throw-in my valueless comments. I am an actor, specializing in voice-over(Correct - shooting-of-my-mouth for money - not in this instance, though.), and I do 3rd. party "rent-a-rep" gigs, demonstrating and selling everything from smoke detectors to computers on weekends(Windows 8 is fine & fun! Don't let the old folks kid ya'.), in a store near you. My general "in store" experience says that "YOU" do not do your research. "YOU" do not read the manual. "YOU" do not take care of your stuff. "YOU" do not do any maintenence. "YOU" only go to websites like this when you have something to complain about - which for "YOU" is most of the time, because of previously mentioned "problems" of "YOUR" own doing! ALL of the rest of us, even when there are problems, which there sometimes are, can see those commenters who at least take the time to try to NOT be "YOU"...

Teddy G. - [...](Yes, my site needs updating. I'll get to it - soon as I finish PW'ing - ha!)

top_rant.text:
I actually bought this from Wal**t.com on sale under $400 with free shipping. Arrived in less than 4 days. Very happy with that part of purchase. Set it all up in no time by myself....less than an hour. I used a pool cover pump to transfer water from my nearby pool into the spa. The pool water is already chemically treated and substantially warmer than water from the hose. It was filled in about 3 hours. Turned on the control panel and everything worked! I was thrilled. Now I just had to wait for the water temp to get up to 98 or so....which would take overnight. (2 degress per hour is accurate). Next morning the spa was 98 degrees. Perfect. Put on the bubbles......it looked great. Appeared very impressive on my deck by the pool. My 8 year old daughter was anxious and wanted to go in....so I let her go in for a little while. She went back and forth from the pool to the spa...numerous times. She got out, I turned off the bubbles and put the cover back on. A little while later I came back outside and heard the control panel beeping. I went over and the entire control panel was lit up. No buttons worked. I reset the panel (basically unplugged it and plugged it back in) and the same thing happened. All icons on panel lit up, beeping, and buttons unresponsive. My wife and 2 other children wanted to go in.....and here I am all bummed out that the thing is broken. I called the Intex customer service....and like others who wrote reviews.....was talking with someone who's primary language was not English. At least I wasn't on hold for more than 5 minutes....and the phone call was over pretty quickly. She just took my contact info, what the problem was, and told me I would receive an email to file my claim. I opted to go through Intex first....as I could always return the entire thing to Wal**rt in the future if I have to. Within 10 minutes I was online and uploaded my receipt an filled out the claim form. The next day I received an email that I would be shipped a new control panel. Later in the day...I got a shipment notification and a FedEx tracking number. VERY impressed with the customer service so far. The only downfall is that the "estimated" delivery date is over a week away.....now I have to decide to drain the spa or not until it arrives. As for the overall experience......if it eventually works as well as it looks....this will have been, even with this hiccup, a great purchase. I have purchased Intex products before....having 2 Intex pools and related intex filter pumps....and have been very happy with their products. Intex is based in California and Canada.....but evidently some, if not all, of their products are imported. If you read the negative reviews....perhaps the people did not properly follow the instructions when filing a claim, or perhaps Intex knows that Wal**rt is a huge retailer of their products compared to smaller vendors on Amazon...and expedites claims. Who knows....but there hasn't been any issues with customer service delaying anything for me. In fact......it only took 1 day to get a FexEx tracking number from my initial claim. We will see....and I will keep posting to this review as things progress.

UPDATE: a week passed and I got my replacement control unit. I had drained the spa....so before I replaced the new control unit...I filled it with my pool water as mentioned earlier. After a few hours it was filled. I replaced the control unit, plugged it in, pushed the power button and presto! A loud beeping noise, all the icons lit up, and nothing working. Reset the control unit numerous times....nothing. Another call to INTEX customer service...phone call answered promptly, and was over in 5 minutes. They will be shipping me a control BASE this time as they think the power supply unit or something electrical in the base is causing the issue. This was yesterday...and I got an email confirmation that my base unit was approved and will be shipped. If all goes well, I will be getting a shipping confirmation today or tomorrow. But then the week wait again...and deciding to empty the spa or not once again. Third time's a charm? We'll see. I have a 90-day return policy with WALM**T...so I will play the game for now and keep my fingers crossed.

UPDATE: been another week and the BASE unit is scheduled to arrive today. Weird thing is that for some reason I decided to reset the spa last night (I kept the water in it)...and the control panel beeped once and started working! I "gently" pushed the heater control up to 100 degrees...and the filter pump turned on. I pushed the bubble button and the thing started bubbling!! I turned off the bubbles and left the thing, checking from my window if it was still lit up every so often. I went outside before bedtime and the temp said 80 degrees. It was still going. This morning I got up, and it had died over the night. Control panel all lit up and unresponsive. What a roller coaster....up and down. I am so anxious for this thing to work. I will hook up the new BASE tonight and give a final do or die update in the next few days. Thumbs up....or back to Walm**t. No more chances with Intex customer support.

UPDATE: BASE unit arrived yesterday and I immediately switched it out with the old one. I also attached the new control panel I received last week. Plugged it in, switched it on and.....everything seems to be working!! The hum from the base is not as loud as the old one, so maybe that's an indication in a positive direction. Water was at 76 degrees...and I set it to 100....which it was this morning when I checked it. Still going. Good. Hopefully this will be my last post. We will give it a try later today.

UPDATE: 5 days later, and everything working well. Tub is great, and it looks as good as it feels. Everyone who sees it wants one. I am keeping my fingers crossed that this will last a few seasons or more.

UPDATE: Been 3 weeks since I hooked up the replacement BASE unit. Everything working fine. Had to clean the filters about 5 days ago, because of an error code indicating the filters needed cleaning/replacement. Last night I had my window open and I heard the spa bubbling. I went outside and sure enough, under the cover, the bubbles were going. Pushed the "bubbles" button numerous times....still going. Pushed the heat button and they stopped. Pushed the heat button again and the bubbles turned on. Now the bubbles go on every time the heat is on. NO GOOD!!! I reset the breaker, etc., and the same problem. I have the original control panel and will hook it up later today, but THREE STRIKES YER OUT!!!. It's only a matter of time this thing will crap out again. GREAT CONCEPT....but poor quality concerning the electronics on this thing. Still have over a month to return to WALM**T....but it looks like when I emtpy it for the winter (or the old control unit doesn't work)...it will be making that "RETURN" trip. COLEMAN just started advertising a spa on Amazon.....I may look into that.

FINAL UPDATE: Hooked up the old original control unit and the same thing. Turn on the heater and the bubbles turn on too. I am so bummed that I am one of the few that isn't having luck with this.....but in the long run, it is probable that the lack of quality concerning the electronics will eventually catch up with a lot of these 5 star reviewers. My spa will be going back to the local Walxxxt in the next few days. Thank heaven for their 90 day return policy. Can't deal with Intex customer service again...even though they will send me new parts....they just crap out.

Ok, Its summer 2015 and Walxxt has it on sale for $329 and free shipping. I must be crazy...but I ordered another one. It is sitting boxed in my garage waiting to be set up. Maybe I will be lucky this time around. It will be set up this weekend and I will post the results. Worst case scenario....another return back to the store. That's the good thing about purchasing it online from Walmxxt....if there is a problem (within return policy), you just bring it back to your local store,,,,no hassle concerning shipping it back to somewhere.

8/10/15: Set up new spa with no problem. (Have had much past experience). Filled with water from hose. Was up to temperature (100 degrees) by the next morning. Been going about 4 days now. Had a BIG pool birthday party for my daughter 2 days ago....kids in and out....many, many people commenting on how cool it is, etc. So far so good. I kept my eye on the kids making sure they didn't push buttons too many times, etc., and was yelling at them if they got too rough. I'm making sure it's babied for a while. Hopefully my over reaction will diminish the longer it keeps going without issues. You would think that Intex would beef up their products since there are so many returns. It costs them an arm and a leg to manufacture and ship out free replacement parts. Using thicker wire or bigger blower/heater would help cut back on most of the problems (In my case its all been electronics). Even if they upped the price $100.

8/27/15: Been going over 3 weeks without issue. Drained it once and refilled with fresh water as the developed a strange smell. Noted in other posts that the rubber/laytex sides will leach out a bit since the product is new. This will go away with time. New water has been in it for 2 weeks and it is still clear without smell. Washed filters out last week and will again soon. Keep a small 1" tab of clorine in the little floatie and test water every 3/4 days. If clorine tests high, i take out floatie for a few days and keep in glass dish by spa. So far so good on this one.

9/21/15: Still working without issue. Did note that those who complained about the sides getting slimey were correct. Even with the chemicals balanced correctly, there is a slight film (rubs off easily) that developes after a period of non-use. Especially on the bottom of the cover pillow that makes contact with the water. Not sure how to correct that problem....but can live with it for now......a quick brushing and its gone.

10/15/15: The spa is still working fine. Getting colder outside, and my pool is covered for the season, so I guess this is a wrap for now. Time to empty it and put it away. Will report back next spring.

February, 2017: Sorry for not getting back sooner! Spa got set up in late Spring of 2016. After inflating and filling with water, turned on the unit and everything lit up correctly. After I pushed the circulation button....nothing. After a few seconds got that familiar beeping noise with the code that indicated pump was not working. Tried a few times.....still nothing. I decided not to panic...and let it sit. After a day or two, I turned on the unit, pushed the circulation button and to my surprise....the pump started working!! Turned on the heater and had no problems at all for the rest of the season. I'm thinking that slight corrosion over the winter prevented the impellers from moving and caused the problem. After sitting for a few days, the water loosened up the impeller. I think. Who knows. At any rate, the spa worked flawlessly for almost 4 months until I packed it away in September. Keeping fingers crossed for next season.

Finale' 8/5/2017: Got it out for 2017 season....everything prepared to inflate it. Control unit turned on and inflated the tub to the correct pressure. Didn't start filling it with water as I didn't have time. Returned later in the day and the damn thing was deflated. Checked everything, hooked back pump and reinflated it. A few hours pass and its soft again. It's out to the curb. Good riddance.

top_rant.text:
I had a HUGE rat and mouse infestation,that just came out of nowhere,then we figured out it had come from a big huge pile of boxes of stuff and trash that had been left from previous owners next door from their storage. When it got cleared away,suddenly,everyone looked for a new home. I was very hesitant in trying this after the mixed reviews. When I say i had an infestation,I am not exaggerating, when I say that I would see them running all over the counters,across curtain rods,under the cabinets,and on top of my cabinets where there was an empty space to put decorations,and I would open my cabinet and the whole family would be looking at me,mom,pop,and junior,and a smaller one,4 at a time that time. I would see them in all areas just EVERYWHERE,IT WAS HORRIFYING.

I was going to use Terminex,but they would have laid traps,poison,and I was so afraid a neighbor cat or my own would pets would get sick,or die,and I didn't want t kill them very much,just wanted them to go back outside,neighbor cats pretty much take care of that stuff anyways,and that is sad enough,and some escape,and at least when they catch one,I know they will not be hurt by poison,and at least I am not to blame.Terminex said they would come out and clear little dead guys for me,and gave a guarantee and they said that this would possibly work,but not for long.

My dog was having a nervous breakdown hunting the rats,she hated them,and even ripped holes in my walls. My cat lounged around watching them like he was watching a T.V show. Both tried to play and seemed to like the little teeny mice and killed a few,of the babies,which made me cry. It was when one baby/young mouse,so teeny finally got on a glue trap I had set,the rats,just pushed them under the door and acted like what did I think ,were they stupid? Well,anyways,a baby mouse got on a glue trap,and it was HORRIFIC.The screams of pain,the squeaks :( I am by the way, an ARA,and I do not want to kill ANYTHING but flies because they like to annoy the crap out of you,BUT I do not want to live with rats and mice,but when the baby was stuck and hurt,my dog actually tried to help it,which made it scream louder. PLEASE PEOPLE: DO NOT USE GLUE TRAPS THEY ARE CRUEL AND HORRIBLE. I cried alot,screamed,and had to go end his little life myself,it was one of the worst things I have ever done,and I still feel bad,there was no other choice,he was tore up. The regular traps thankfully did not work either,as that would have traumatized me,so badly.

So,I decided that I would try this product,after looking extensively for a long time,I decided this was the one. I had a cheap one in my room,that i found did at least stop them from invading in here,but otherwise,I was pretty terrified to leave my room. So I get your product,and I wait. And I wait. And I wait. No change at all. THEN!! A MIRACLE!!!

Suddenly,just out of the blue,I would say 3-3 and 1/2 weeks in,I went out there and to my surprise,NOTHING. No-one scurrying off ,no-one scaling walls and curtain rods to get back into their homes-MY CABINETS,walls,and under the sinks.I thought,this is a fluke. Ran got a drink,and raced back to my room,Faye searching desperately ,as she was certain there must be one somewhere. This went on for a week. I did not believe. Faye did not believe. The cat was like,now what am I supposed to do,I have nothing to watch on "T.V"

So I bought them on may 5th,got them a few days later,and I would say by the end of June,they had cleared out. Weirdness. Faye still did not and does not believe. She is slowly forget her trauma of having to protect me,as she did not like them for making me shriek. The cat has found other entertainment,and is back to destroying my things,and slapping everything from every surface,especially my drinks.

Now,I had a couple rooms closed off. One bedroom is a box room,and one is just closed and fairly empty,and it was there that Faye had previously torn through the wall in the bathroom,by the tub near the empty closed room,because she just knew there was one in there,and was determined to get it out.Of course,the cat and dog ALWAYS go to the bathroom,to make sure that I am not eating in there,or doing anything fun without them,plus they are in love with me,so I think that is why the bathroom was always all clear,except I worried why had Faye torn the hole in the wall before,if there was nothing there.

This is the only bad thing since they cleared out,and I had never had them come into the bathroom before. I walked in there,flipped on the light,and saw a rat,just sitting there,looking very confused,who actually was a cuter rat than the others,not that grey kind,but still terrifying,and we both scared the crap out of each other and ran the same way-OUT,I was running backwards and fell and got hurt,and he ran over my foot,OMG,and Faye gave chase,barking "I TOLD YOU HE WAS IN HERE!" and I have never seen him again. I believe that was two weeks ago.

I am afraid the Terminex guys will be proven right and they will return,but grow less afraid every day,as I believe he was just a straggler left behind. I think that one reason they take so long to go,is they must find a way to get their babies out,and find a place to re-locate,I really do. Why else would it take so long? So that is 4 months now I think that i have had an all clear,except the straggler that almost killed me,who has apparently vacated. So I do give 5 stars for this product,and I do think though that I need one more,for the back part of the house,as I do fear they will be in those rooms. I have one in the front,and one in the middle,and the cheapie one in my room. I have my bedroom master bath blocked off,and am afraid to go in there,and am afraid to go into those two locked up rooms,even though I do not hear ANY sounds from them,like I used to ALL THE TIME.

Their vacating the house was either an act of God,or your Pest offense POBD-1-01 model. I hope you will please still have a couple left for me when I am able to buy a couple more,I KNOW they work. I am giving such a long detailed story,A. Because I am a writer and write stories,and do this with just about everything I write,and B. I saw far too many negative feedbacks,and put a leap of faith into your product,decided that they were probably like me,would want an immediate result,and I did hope for one,but you said how long it takes and the leap of faith paid off.You simply may never know what it was like for me,I felt like I was living in a nightmare,so scared of them,knowing they were re-producing,and knowing I could not EVER risk my pets lives or my neighbors.I think of it every day,how very thankful I am to not be scared any more.

I will be HAPPY to have anyone talk to me,that does not believe this thing works,I am a real person,and I had a real home invasion,and I am serious,they could even call me. So much safer then traps and poison,and honestly,you may not see dead rats and mice laying around as proof,but it cleared them in the same length of time or less than Terminex said they could.Nothing against Terminex,but this is a MUCH safer way to do it. (UPDATE May 2015 (Below)
We bought a lot of rural acreage fairly recently, encompassing a whole Mountain Top and the valleys around it. We immediately began clearing the dense trees around our home since the previous owner had let trees grow VERY near the house, with many of the trees leaning OVER the house dangerously due to strong winds from storms. Clearing the land around the house, as well as the pasture area for our horses, has kept my Stihl Chainsaws busy! However, it quickly became obvious that we needed a good Limb-Saw with a long reach also!

I spent a long time looking at Limb-Saws and reading hundreds of reviews on the web. I was thinking I wanted a gas model, yet I needed a limb-saw light enough for my wife to use also (She has NO fear of hard work, or of getting dirty! As petite as she is, she will still grab any tool she's been trained with, and use it with glee! She scares me at times!)... I kept coming back to this electric Limb-Saw, and the reviews finally sold me on it!

As is common in the Rural South, it seems like we're constantly fighting back the plant life in the Spring/Summer/Fall! Limbs seem to grow out over our long driveway almost overnight at times; This Pole-Saw knocks them back though!

This Limb-Saw is everything they advertise it to be! Many other limb-saws we looked at boasted a long "Reach", but we quickly found out that in Advertising Terms there is a big difference between "Length" and "Reach"... "Length" is the actual length of the Saw, whereas "Reach" is the saw's length PLUS your height, PLUS the length of your arms over your head. The other limb-saws we looked at seem to assume an "Average Height" of a person to be about 6 feet tall! Those saws also seemed to hide the actual "length" of the Saws, and ONLY advertise an unrealistic (for many people) "Reach"! That confusing trickery is not used with this saw though! It's got the actual reach advertised assuming you're over 4 feet tall with your arms over your head! As it says in the description, the "actual length" of the limb-saw itself is just under 10 feet with the (included) extension, and so the "Reach" is EASILY over 14 feet if you're big enough to use a saw at all ;). I'm just a touch over 6 feet tall, and I add at least another foot to that when holding this saw over my head, so I easily "reach" limbs well over 17+ feet from the ground! My Wife is petite, but at 5'4", she reaches up to hit limbs at 16+ feet comfortably! Compare that to other saws that boast a "12 foot Reach" (Which seems to be a very commonly advertised "Reach"), and you realize that the (unadvertised) "length" of many other saws is actually only about 6 feet! When you step back after cutting a high limb with *this* saw though, you're amazed how high up it actually was!

I was also amazed at the power of this thing! When you lay it on a limb and pull the trigger, it BITES and begins throwing out small chunks of wood! It just seems to pull itself through whatever you lay it on to cut, at any height it'll reach! I expect that from my Gas Powered Stihl Chainsaws, but I didn't expect it from an electric Limb-Saw!

3 things I'd absolutely advise for using this Limb-Saw:
* Hit the web and read up on how to PROPERLY cut limbs! It makes a LOT of difference if you cut them properly!
* Keep the Chain Sharp! I touch up the chain on ours after each battery we kill.
* Get the better batteries and a Quick-Charger, and get at least two batteries if you plan on using the saw for more than a few occasional limbs!! I can't really give you a time estimate on battery life, because it's highly dependent on what TYPE of limbs you're cutting. Cutting large hard-wood Oak limbs will kill the batteries much faster than cutting smaller soft Pine limbs.

We've cleared the overhanging limbs from the full length of the driveway (Well over a Quarter mile!) since we bought this saw, as well as limbing the trees that we've left standing as we cleared more property; we also use it to "Shape Up" the Leyland Cypresses on the property, AND for reaching into dense Briar patches to clear them out without getting all scratched up... There have been NO problems with the saw in the several months in which we've used it almost daily! My Wife uses the saw without the extra extension pole (included in the package), for cutting up limbs and saplings, etc, before dragging them to brush pile. If you use it for a while, it's going to feel heavy pretty soon, but that's simply unavoidable with something this long, lifting it and holding it way over your head, repeatedly, while cutting limbs! But it DOES cut the limbs! We've cut everything from Pine to very hard Oak with no problems. The power is impressive, especially if you're expecting it to be an anemic "Electric Saw".

As much use as we've gotten from this Limb-Saw already, if it broke today I'd be back here at Amazon buying another one within an hour! That's about the highest praise I can give it.

UPDATE: Since I remember how frustrating it was searching for a Limb Saw online, and all the uncertainty, confusion, and anxiousness we felt looking at all the different models out there, I wanted to update this review now that we've used this Limb-Saw almost daily over the Summer clearing more of our land (the clearing never seems to end! ;)... The Black & Decker Limb-Saw is still going strong! The body of the saw has taken some abuse from hard use, but the "damage" is confined to routine scratches and normal discoloration from dirt, sap, oil, etc... It's been dropped several times, and uncountable limbs have hit it as they fell (knocking the saw out of our hands sometimes). The Chain and "Sword" (the section that the chain rides on) has been pinched HARD many times by big limbs and small logs, etc... Nothing has broken, the chain is still in great shape, the Saw is as strong as the day we bought it, and we have NO complaints! My wife got the saw pinched so tightly by a large oak limb last week that I had to get out my climbing spikes and drag one of my gas powered Stihl Chainsaws up the tree to free the Limb-Saw (It was S-T-U-C-K!)... but again, there was NO damage to the saw! It seems like one neighbor or another drives up almost every weekend to borrow the limb-saw for an hour or so, and several of them have bought their own now after borrowing ours a few times. That works out very well since one neighbor drops off his "Gravely" "Dense Brush Cutter" for us to use on undergrowth while he borrows this Limb-Saw to trim up limbs and such on his adjoining property.

Looking back now, as much as we've used this saw (and use is increasing now that the weather is cooling off some), I keep thinking that I should have bought a Stihl Gas Powered Limb-Saw due to all the use and abuse we've heaped on this poor thing; We didn't realize how much we'd actually use a limb-saw when we bought this one, and we've used it WAY WAY above what I'd consider "Light Home Use"... but honestly, if there's a Gas Powered Limb-Saw with the same reach, and the same light weight of this saw, I haven't seen it yet! Again, we have NO complaints, and would buy another one of these immediately if our current one breaks or wears out! Maybe we just got the Golden Limb-Saw of the production run, but I don't think that's the case since our neighbors that have bought them also don't seem to have any problems with theirs either.

I'm sorry this review is so long, but I hope it helps other people who are struggling with the same worry and frustration we went through while trying to decide on which limb-saw to buy! Let me say again though, please re-read the "3 things I'd absolutely advise for using this Limb-Saw" above; Follow that advice and you'll get a LOT more use from this saw, as well as being a LOT happier!

(UPDATE: MAY 2015) It's now MAY of 2015, and this Limb-Saw and it's ORIGINAL Batteries are STILL Going STRONG, as good as the day we bought it! We were SO impressed with this saw, that we bought the Matching little "Electric Chain Saw" that uses the same batteries.. My wife STILL uses the Limb Saw, AND the Little Electric Chainsaw, almost EVERY DAY! Not ONE SINGLE PROBLEM from EITHER saw in almost 3 years!!!

top_rant.text:
I am incredibly disappointed with the flowtron bug zapper. I bought it because of the product claim that the flowtron BK-40D would annihilate 99.9% of all mosquitoes (plus a ton of other bugs), living in/located within/or just having the unfortunate luck of being in the full acre surrounding the flowtron. We have two acres, so I thought this would be the perfect weapon to fight the hordes of mosquitoes that unfortunately infiltrate our property each year. I figured I’d get two and that would take care of our entire property. HOW EXCITING 🤗!!! However, I was at least smart enough to just try one flowtron to begin with until I got a real understanding of how the thing worked, and to obviously get some first-hand experience before completely falling for the hype. (I should also mention that this zapper also promised some other “to-good-to-be-true type things, but they mostly relied on purchasing some other accessories before they’d be proven to be true or (more likely) disappoint the masses.) However, I decided to just stick with the basic mosquito massacre model, so I went on Amazon and ordered the flowtron BK-40D. Based on everything I read about this machine, I truly believed I was in for nothing short of a miracle, but boy was I wrong!!!! Not only was it pretty much identical to every other mosquito zapping machine I’ve ever seen/owned, but the thing came with some pack of liquid or something to stick on the machine in order to attract a cult of kool-aid drinking bunnies or something like that...oh wait, now that I think about it, I think it was actually for getting giant jumping spiders to commit suicide, (not even joking about that one)! I mean not really , or at least not specifically for euthanizing Charlotte and her chubby family members, but I promise you, it did come with a packet meant to do some sort of major damage to some type of living creature. But, unfortunately, the instructions I received about attaching the packet, slash using the original product as intended were so incredibly hard to comprehend, I had to eventually give up. And there’s even more to this tragedy...PLOT TWIST!!! How exciting, right?!? Not right...the stupid thing broke within a couple of weeks. And on top of that (yes, there’s more!!!), I made sure to study the machine’s success each and every day. (To do this, one must look at the zapper with one’s own eyes and determine whether or not the zapper is actually zapping. (A pile of bugs underneath the machine and pieces of crusty wing stuck to the machine are exactly the kind of evidence you’d be needing as proof that the zapper is zapping.) So anyway, each morning I’d go out expecting to see evidence that the machine was working and doing the job it had been given. And each day, I’d pass by the zapper with a familiar pang of disappointment. No piles of dead mosquitoes...no collage of crusty wrinkled wings, ...just a flowtron BK-40D, hanging there and mocking the excitement I once had that we’d “finally get a handle on that annoying mosquito problem”. Just like our daughter’s pool, cowering in the background, green with algae, mocks the excitement our children once had to play in the 81,892 square feet of what was once our yard, but during spring and summer months, it is better known as the bordello of sucking blood and the house of encephalitis, malaria and ill repute. AKA the 81,892 square foot breeding ground for one of the most annoying and vile creatures God could have ever allowed on planet Earth. But I digress. As I was saying, the flowtron was not living up to the promises it made about killing all the mosquitoes within a one mile radius, let alone killing the ones hanging around our back porch. It was pathetic. I mean, I literally watched a mosquito fly around the flowtron, and then stop and land on it. And I swear, I saw that thing laughing hysterically and flipping me off, just taking a break on what was supposed to be the literal depiction of a mosquito killing machine...yet here it is, just hanging there, available to these freaking mosquitoes for their rest and relaxation whenever they just want to take a breather, ya know?!? I mean c’mon!!! At that point, I was just about at the last straw if you will...AND THEN IT HAPPENED... I wanna say this was maybe two and a half, maybe three weeks after I shelled out the what, maybe $50, $60 or something like that to buy what I thought was going to be our miracle solution, the flowtron BK-40D......AND THE DAMN THING BROKE! I kid you not. THAT’S RIGHT: $60, 3 WEEKS AFTER BUYING IT, IT FREAKING BROKE!!! SOMETHING ELECTRICAL THAT ONLY A SPECIALIST CAN FIX!!! This machine that was a, yes I know, “zapper“, was still supposed to be the top-of-the-line, best-in-its-class, can’t get any better, #1 Ever Made bug zapper, specializing in the supposedly humane method of exterminating mosquitoes. And it broke after three weeks! The light turned to a faint purple when it was plugged in and it was no longer able to injure, mame or kill any bug of any kind. I called the number on the website and no one was able to help me. I could try to have them replace it, but they wanted me to pay for the new one up front and then send them back the “old one” (though in my mind 3 weeks will never in any way be considered “old”.) If the problem with the “old one” isn’t covered under the very very extremely limited warranty, then I am stuck with having to pay $60 for each zapper, $120 total. But, if the problem is covered under the very very limited warranty, then I would get a refund of the $60 I just paid for the replacement zapper.

After receiving this explanation as basically my only option (unless I wanted to go to a private electrician, mechanic or engineer to fix whatever the problem was with the original, and then pay them whatever their fee is to fix it...YEAH RIGHT!!!) So basically, I realized that this was just going to be a lose-lose-lose situation for me. I’d lose my money, I’d lose the zapper and I’d LOSE MY MIND!!! So I figured I’d just stick with the L/L/L plan, basically because It’s the cheapest option, and I didn’t feel like being ripped off by anyone else. So basically, the zapper (Flowtron BK-40D) SUCKS ASS! The company who makes/sells it and provides the customer service on the worst warranty in history SUCKS ASS! But, most of all, mosquitoes SUCK MAJOR ASS!!!! So, I 100% WOULD NOT RECOMMEND you buy the Flowtron BK-40D, unless you feel like getting ripped off or if you have some weird fetish for being ripped off and it’s cheaper to buy the flowtron than some weirdly specific prostitute, then I would say to go ahead and steer clear of buying this crappy machine now or in the future, unless they make a version in say 20 years that doesn’t SUCK ASS, but I think we all know that’s not very likely! So what I’m saying is, I bought this product, it SUCKS ASS, I 100% DO NOT RECOMMEND the Flowtron BK-40D, I am giving this product 1 star, oh yeah, and MOSQUITOES SUCK ASS TOO!!! Thanks for reading my review!!! Have a wonderful day!!!

If there was a 4 1/2 star option that is what I would truly give it because of the few things I find wrong with the sauna. However, that being said 5 BIG STARS is what I will give it!!!
My sauna arrived faster than I thought it would. The driver actually made sure the pallet was as close as he could get it to the garage doors. So right from the beginning this seemed to be a great experience. We joked around a bit regarding how he could use a sauna after his hard day of delivering heavy cartons all over CT and other states close by and I offer him a free sauna if he would help me build it! Of course he passed on the idea and went on his way and I had a shit eating grin on my face looking at the three boxes that contained one of the things I had on my bucket list for so long and it finally could be checked off. I could have waited for my son to help me bring it all in the house to the spot I was going to put it but I could just not wait for 6 hours to go by so I decided I would open the boxes one at a time and carry each piece in by myself. After I got them all in I took out the (EASY) to follow directions and started to build my bucket list sauna. The directions we clear as a bell and I truly have time with following directions because for some reason I find most are written so poorly. After reading negative reviews about the instructions difficult to follow I found that not to be the case for me and I know it may be difficult to believe it but I built the whole thing all by myself! Some of the pieces were a bit heavy and I am a small guy only 5'7" and 165lbs. but I managed by myself. The door was the hardest to carry alone but even that I managed to do. I think I wanted it so bad I had the adrenaline rush thing going on!!! I do remember somewhere reading that you do not need tools to build this sauna however that is not the case you do need two and they are a screwdriver both flat and phillips and that is it!!! True story. It clips together and lines up really good. Yes I have read the reviews where things did not line-up but this is my review and they did.
After it was all built I cleaned up and wiped down both the inside and outside with a slightly damp cloth. There was a tiny bit of saw dust on some of the pieces so I would recommend having a vacuum at hand just to clean it up as you go along. It is minimal but I didn't want to track it all over the house.
Now for the test - plug it in and turn it on, crossed my fingers and said a Hail Mary that it heated up and worked correctly and VOILA it did!!! Oh happy day!!! Very happy day! #ohhappyday
The control panel has:
Power on/off button - High/low setting either in F or C - Timer from 1 min. to 59 mins.
There is a light switch with a small light just over the control panel more so you can see the panel rather than for seeing in the sauna itself as you would really not be able to read very well with just this light. And there is an USB cable so you can attach your mobile device and listen to the music or what ever you choose that will come from the two speakers on each side of the light socket* with a small platform for you to rest your mobile device on. There is also a cable to the right of the control panel for you to add the Oxygen Ionizer if you choose to add this at a later date or when you purchase your sauna I decided to wait on this and would use it as a treat for a later date. There is also a socket* for the Chromotherapy Light if you want to purchase that extra as well. Again I decided I would add these features at a later date.
However, I did purchase the set of backrests for my sauna and I am so glad I did. They also work great! I would suggest you do a search for these as the prices are all over the place and I actually found them listed on Ebay at a seller for only $42.00/set with FREE shipping. I love them as they fit the small of my back just perfect and besides they really look cool when in place. So because I liked them so much I decided to purchase the Neck Rests as well and purchased them on the JNH website as I found them to be the lowest price there. However, don't be in a rush and shop around for better deals if you can find them. That is what I did and my search efforts brought me to purchase from those I stated above however you may do better with your own search. Also if you see the sauna somewhere else for a lower price call JNH and tell them and they will look it up and meet the price for you! I think this company is GREAT!!!
My first experience was wonderful as well as all to follow since I purchased my sauna and if you are on the edge of if you should or shouldn't I would suggest you GO FOR IT. I truly don't think you will regret it.
My PROS are listed in the review and my CONS are as follows:
1. There is no weather stripping around the door to seal the hot air in place.
2. My control panel is slightly off center (this is my OCD kicking in place)
3. I feel the heater under the seat could be longer to provide more heat down there. There is room enough for the company to make a panel about 6" - 8" longer and I think it would make a difference. It's not cold by all means but I understand what the others are saying in their reviews regarding a cold spot at the floor level. If the company builds a longer panel for that section I would have no problem in purchasing it to up grade the level of heat down there.
4. I have an Iphone and the phone heated up too much and would not allow the music to play for more than 30 min. as it said "the phone needed to cool down. This is not a fault of the sauna it's just too much heat for the phone. However, I was able to remedy that problem by reversing my roof panel of the sauna an putting the air vents to the back instead of the front and I feed the cable through the airvent to the outside of the sauna and now I can listen all day if I wanted to without the phone over heating. And you don't loose heat with through the vent being open as you can close it just to the cord and the opening is so small that I doubt you are loosing but a bit of heat. Perhaps JHN willl feed the cord through the sauna in a different place so this won't have to be done for future customers.
5. NOTHING MORE AT THIS TIME - NOT TO SAY IT'S NOT GOING TO CHANGE DOWN THE LINE BUT RIGHT NOW THE CONS ARE SO MINOR. THEREFORE THE 4 1/2 STAR STATEMENT AT THE BEGINNING OF MY REVIEW.
I called JNH and told them my concerns and spoke with a few different people. EVERYONE was just wonderful to speak with and all are concerned that the customer is realy happy and taken care of. I mean they really care and listen to what you have to say. For the few of you that have stated that costomer service is bad - WHAT THE HECK ARE YOU TALKINNG ABOUT??? They were great to me and cared about what I have to say. Of course there are those that no matter what in life happens to them have to complain and have a fit over the slightest thing and call up so upset that they treat the person at the other end like s--- so what do you expect??? No one should have to listen to someone being out of control no matter what the problem. Sometimes we forget that that person did not make the product themselves, they didn't do it to you, they are there to help you and listen to your concerns and try to make things right for you. All the yelling and meaness in the world will NOT get you anywhere. They are doing the best they can and I get your fustrations when things you pay a lot for are either broken or not working correctly. It's a bummer yes, but they personally did not do it to you so give them a break and hold your cool and explain the problem with kindness toward them and I am sure you will get a lot more from your complaint/s. I personally had all good experiences and Alex gave me the best customer service possible. He not only was kind and curitious but he truly listend to my concerns I stated in a kind and nice manner and therefore I assure you I was well taken care of. THANK YOU! Alex you are truly an asset to the JNH company and I only hope they reconize you for it.
If things change over time I will update my review but for now I am a happy camper with another bucket list item checked off before I leave this part of lifes journey. #ohhappyday.
PLEASE note that this review is for the JNH Lifestyles Joyous 2 person infrared sauna and not any other saunas in the JHN collection.
