top_rant.text:
Note - this will not be able to show as a verified purchase from Amazon. I'm an avid Amazon consumer, but this round, we went directly to the company to make sure we got the correct 4x4 base and automatic vent that would work with the structure... I don't believe, at the time, I could find a 4x4 base size)

The finished product is fabulous… the actual construction process was difficult. I’m really good at following directions and have built many a piece of furniture etc. When I read the reviews prior to buying, I was telling myself ‘pffft… I’m better that those people on the internet… how challenging can building it be’. When I open the product, I carefully took everything apart, verified the count of the nicely labeled pieces (that I had everything) and got everything ‘organized’. I started on a Saturday evening. Knowing how people struggled on the assembly, I decided to keep track how long it took me to do everything.

I logged at 10:05 PM on bottom of page 2 of the instructions “Woohoo !!” (I was feeling pretty good – got the door done).

Page 3 10:43 PM “Woohoo” (Still great – got the window vent assembled) Went to bed feeling how good I was

Sunday morning, 8:21 AM, back at it. Open up page 4 and start working away.

Was doing pretty good, but got stuck dealing with the ‘bastard’ piece (W02) on page 7 … it thought it would conquer me… but I got it beat at 12:35PM (wow…that took me four hours for four pages… okay… not feeling so cocky… I can understand why those reviews were notating the hard assembly)

Continued working – pages 8 to 11…. 2 hours later (I have @!#!@! notated at the bottom of the page 2:37PM) Part of the issue was I had two pieces labeled the same, but they were indeed different … it had to do with LO1D and LO1F… it wasn’t until I got to page 10 to use LO1F that I realized that LO1F had an extra hole that was needed for the top.. but I had installed it (as I had two LO1D labeled) as LO1D pages 8 to 10 with the piece with the hole way back on page 8…. Grrrr… had to carefully take things a part to switch out what I thought was the correct LO1D with the correct one. I did feel proud of myself that I was able to resolve the problem and that I didn’t give up… decided to take a break from it…it’s Sunday after all, time for an afternoon nap).

8 PM Sunday night with a fresh mind, started back on page 12… “I can do this”… oh no… what… looks like whoever wrote the instruction from page 1 to 11 decided they didn’t want to give any more details on the small parts (the nuts, bolts, etc). Then flipping the pages after that I realize that starting from page 12.. it’s literally more like a ‘screenshot’ drawing of what the piece should look like. The main ‘long pieces’ are referenced, but there is not detail on the small parts to put it together. All of a sudden, I realize I need to look at the prior pages (1-11) over and over again to try to determine what was the likely build/nuts/bolts that the ‘screenshot’ picture was telling me. This made an already challenging process seem nearly impossible. I have to admit, my husband came out several times to keep me from taking a crowbar to the entire project. Finished the ‘bastard’ at 12:02 AM…. Four hours later. With my ‘guessing’ at bolts/screws I did find I was two bolts and screws short at the end and had to go to Home Depot later that week to find a replacement. (I had a screw loose…. Funny)

I didn’t build the base, the automatic vent opener, and add the rubber strip (J04) until a later date (for sake of sanity). (Just clarifying that the hours/days notated above in didn’t include this piece of construction)

The base was a cake walk to build (especially after the weekend of ‘hell’)… the rubber strip (J04) went in nicely. The automatic vent opener had the same instructions flaw. There were more pieces/parts in the package that what I could identify from the Assembly instructions. Fortunately, I googled several other sites and zoomed in on literal assembly screen shots to determine how to assemble. My third email will contain 5 shots of the two pieces that I couldn’t identify what to do with. (It wasn’t clear to me which was the correct ‘split pin’ … there were two pieces that seem like a ‘split pin’… I figured out from googling)

Okay, after all that… am I happy with the product?. Assembly process set aside… the greenhouse itself is a solid piece (I would recommend anyone considering in purchasing that the greenhouse is much more solid with the base attached… makes it easier to move two as you have a solid ‘square’ held together by the base). Since we were putting on top of newly laid pavers, I built the base ‘stakes’ UP (instead of down into the ground). They stick up a little bit, but I figured it would be extra protection for the corners for when people are garden equipment is going by. (If you look at the four bottom corners, you will see that the stake is indeed up). The stakes are what hold the base together and are intended to be in the ground for extra security. We get pretty breezy, we simply put a small paver square on the inside of each corner just in case… so far, it has been rain and wind sturdy.

Now, what I’m using it for is probably different than what anyone has considered using a greenhouse for. We have a problem with coyotes in our area… plus, with our recent backyard remodel, we were getting rid of our patio cover for the time being (the expense of adding pavers left no money for a patio cover)… so I was trying to find a solution for my 4 lb. poodle to have a ‘bathroom’ area where she would be protected from coyotes and the rain. Before our patio remodel, we had an AKC kennel (see picture)… even with a partially covered patio, her piddle pads would get wet when rain blew in the wind sideways. After thinking thru various solutions (I considered a small enclosed shed… what I didn’t want it to be dank and dark for her)… I came across the greenhouse idea.

Your greenhouse is a perfect solution!!! We had a doggie door installed in our master bedroom. Now our poodle can go out whenever she want (day OR night)… rain or shine… and have a protected area to go the bathroom that is coyote proof. (As for coyote proof, we don’t keep any food or water in the structure (which could attract coyotes further)… nor are we using the greenhouse to keep our dog in during the day (remember it is a greenhouse, and though it has an automatic vent that helps release heat… it can get a bit warm in there… plus, for a larger dog, you would have to consider attaching the greenhouse to the house… we only have a lean and we did NOT attach it to the house.. just ‘leaning’ against the house … and a four pound poodle isn’t going to be able to move it (side note – with the base, the greenhouse is not flush to the house… I bought vinyl door strips from Home Depot and installed it along the top to help cut down on rain entering… of course, it is still under the eaves))…. In the end, it is truly a bathroom area… she goes out, does her business, and immediately comes back inside. We found two large plastic garden trays from Amazon and lined them with piddle pads. We replace the piddle pads now and then (she stays cleaner since she isn’t tracking in grass/dirt etc.)

PLUS… the greenhouse is nice looking (I included a picture of our old setup… which worked for what we needed… but I wanted something nicer looking and something more rain proof).

Anyways, I took the time to take pictures and my experience so that others could learn/benefit and consider this as a doggie option. Would I buy it again, yes… would I want to construct it again… well… I probably could go faster the second round (plus have a bottle of something good to cut the frustrations).

Thanks so much for reading …
top_rant.title: Great product ... using as rain and coyote structure for doggie bathroom area... but assembly difficult!!!

top_rant.text:
I did a lot of research before settling on the Broilmaster Qrave Q3X. I had to because the manufacturer does not do a good job at conveying the specifications for their products. I contacted and worked with 3 different dealers because I kept getting conflicting information from each. Meanwhile, the manufacturer's brochure and website didn't provide basic information. Examples:

- What is the cooking area? I got three different answers. 500, 695 and 399 sq. inches (it's about 500).
- What are the low and high interior temperatures? Again, three different answers. Don't know, 150-400, and 220-500 deg. F. (With the right burner on low and the top down, the lowest temperature was 267 deg.F. And 620 deg. F with both burners on high and lid closed).
- Can I sear a steak or a burger? No, Yes and No. (If no, "Why does the mfr. show steaks on their website?" No idea).
- If I buy a standard SS grille, can I replace the "screen" style grille? Don't know, No, and No. (Real answer? Nobody knows.

The grill arrived in one large box, delivered by UPS. Once I open the box I found that everything, except the base, is packed inside the grill body. Nice, except it looked like someone sat on the contents. The bucket, control panel shield and heat shield were all bent. :( The good news is that I was able to bend them all back into reasonable good shape.

Assembly: When I first looked at the parts, I though "I'll have this baby ready in an hour." No such luck. Assembly took exactly 3 hours.

As I assembled the grill I was struck at the poor tolerances. I got everything together, yes. But things don't align properly and some screws ended up going in cockeyed. Also, the base and tower don't integrate very nicely. The tower is rectangular whereas it slips into a curved depression in the plastic base, leaving a gap that will collect crap over time.

Following are my annotations to the instructions provided.

Base & tower assembly instructions:

The brochure states that it is for Models PCB1-3 and DCB1-3, however, there is no indication anywhere that informed which base was in the box. The base I received does not look like the drawing on this brochure, but it does match the photo on the website. The front is straight rather than curving over the wheels.

page 8 - The drawing shows that the tower is curved, but it's not. A gap between the curved plastic base and the straight metal panels will end up collecting god knows what crap over time. Bad design detail.

page 9 - Cart Assembly - The components have poor tolerances. I had to substitute the screws and nuts that hold the rear brace because the resulting hole (where three pieces of metal come together) was too small for the screws provided. Not a big deal, but products from China are better made.

page 9 - The screws to hold the grill body to the base went in cockeyed because the holes didn't align.

Owners Manual Q3X-2:

page 14 - Install Hose and Regulator (For LP Models) say that pipe thread should be wrapped with silicone tape. I assume it should, as it's a gas line. But I'm not an engineer. I wrapped it, just in case.

page 15 - Heat Shield/Wind Shield - Instructions should clarify that this piece of sheetmetal merely (and remarkably) sits loose on the floor of the grill body.

page 15 - Igniter Wire - First, the drawing is misleading because it appears as if this is a separate component. At first I thought that if hadn't been included. In fact, it comes attached to the burner. Second, the instructions say that the bushing doesn't apply to this model, but it does. Third, the instructions should advise buyer to tag one of the wires because later, when you push the two wires through the tiny hole, to outside of the grill body, you can't tell which wire is which. They look identical.

page 16 - Install the Burner - #3 states "Slide the venturi on to the valve assembly." However, there is no explanation as to what or where "the venturi" is. Nor is there any description of what "the valve assembly" is. Remarkably, once secured in place (see #4 and #5), the holes of the two burner pipes didn't physically connect to the two pipes extending from the regulator assembly. My burners didn't work until I manually wrestled the two burner pipes to engage with the regulator pipes. i.e. so they stick into the burner pipes.

page 16 - Connect Igniter Wires and Install Battery - Two comments:

1. The igniter is a small, black rectangular gizmo attached to the underside of the control panel, which is not explained. i.e. behind the igniter push button assembly. It is not a separate piece, as suggested in the diagram.
2. The battery must be installed the opposite of what the instructions state. i.e. insert the battery negative side down. I figured this out because my igniter initially did not work.

page 19 - Assemble Drain Pan - Instructions should be more clear that the pipe and both elbows should be threaded and tightened together and installed on the drain pan BEFORE placing the drain pan into the grill body. You will need a pair of channel lock pliers to properly tighten these pieces.

page 19 - Install the Drain Pan Assembly - I was incredulous when I discovered that the drain valve handle ends up pointing down in the bucket. I tried installing the drain valve up side down, but you can't because there isn't sufficient space between the handle and the grill body to thread in the valve. So you handle ends up in the bucket. Sorry, but this is poorly designed.

page 20 - Air shutter adjustment - I have no idea what Broilmaster is trying to convey here. There is no drawing provided, nor any description of the "venturri air shutter(s)," so I have no idea which is the "shutter set screw" that must be loosened. Also, it states "Close the air shutter to the minimum opening," but there is no indication as to where or what it is.

Conclusion: Overall, I'm please with the grill, although I have not yet actually cooked anything on it. I try to remember to post a follow up. My greatest concern was that the highest temperature would be too low, but that isn't a problem as it reaches 620 deg. F. I am disappointed, however, that the description on Broilmaster's website is not true, "So when your grandfather’s secret pork shoulder recipe calls for 200 to 220 degrees for 14 hours, that’s precisely what you’ll get." Not me. The lowest temperature I got was 267 deg. F.

I hope people will find this helpful.

8-11-18 FOLLOW UP:

I've had some interesting evenings with the Broilmaster Qrave. The inaugural event involved 1-1/2" think prime boneless ribeye steaks. I placed the grates in the high position and preheated the grill for 15 minutes on high. I let the steaks sit on the counter for an hour and then salted them a couple of minutes before placing them on the grill. I was somewhat disappointed that when they hit the hot grill surface, there wasn't the sort of sizzle that I would get on my now retired DuKane grill. Hmm. I closed the cover and set a timer for 7 minutes. Peeked and decided to wait another 3 minutes before flipping. The steak was definitely seared, although not as crisped and dark as I am accustomed to. Next, I installed my thermometer probe into the meat and waited until the steak reached an internal temperature of 135 degrees F. I removed it and flipped it over on to a cutting board to sit for several minutes. The sear on the second side was even less than the first side, but the steak was cooked to a perfect, juicy pink.

The following day I prepared the grill the same way for a couple of 1/2" thick pieces of chicken breast that I had seasoned with garlic powder, salt and black pepper. Obviously, I cooked it for a shorter time, but there was effectively no sear to speak of. The chicken was moist, but overall it was a disappointment. There is something about those slightly burned edges that lend a lot of flavor to chicken. My wife suggested that next time I should place the grills at the low setting.

The bonfire:

A couple of night later we had friends over for dinner. I decided to try grilling ribeye's again. I bought the same kind, from the same store. They were beautiful. After they were on the grill for about 5 minutes I decided to check on them and carefully opened the lid about an inch to have a peak. YIKES!!! The steaks were in flames! As in "a lot of flames." I quickly open the lid and flipped them over to the other side of the grill. Almost instantly, fat dripped on to the pan below and burst into flames again. I quickly turned left burner off and lowered the right burner to low and moved the steaks back to the left side. The flames died, and the second side of the steaks was now seared. So far so good. ;) I shoved in the temperature probe into one of the steaks and closed the lid again. When the thermometer registered 135 degrees F. I pulled them off and let them sit for 5 minutes. Everyone agreed that these steaks were outstanding. Moist, pink and so flavorful. I just don't know if I'll be able to pull this stunt off again. (The following morning I deleted the infrared sear burner from my Amazon basket.)

Last night we made pizza. Nothing fancy. I put our rectangular pizza stone into the grill and pre-heated for 20 minutes on high, while I prepared a pizza consisting of Trader Joe's pizza dough, TJ's pizza sauce, shredded mozzarella, slices of green pepper, chopped onions, a few Italian olives (chopped), red pepper flakes, a good helping of fennel seeds and topped off with a sprinkling of Parmesan cheese. The built-in thermometer registered 525 deg. F. I slid the pizza off the peel, closed the lid and baked it for 10 minutes. The outcome was shear perfection. The crust and cheese were both beautifully browned. And the crust was nicely crispy.

Cooking summary: Clearly, I am still learning how to use this somewhat unusual grill, but I was really taken aback at the fire. The idea of this grill is that you don't get flare-ups. I have to tell you that when liquid fat from a steak hits that hot pan below, it's not a flare-up ... it's a veritable bonfire.

Other comments/observations:

Turning on the grill is hit or miss. I open the lid, turn on one burner to high and depress the igniter button for a few seconds. For some reason, half the time it doesn't work.

A huge improvement would be if the grill had a way to see the flames. My old DuKane grill has a view port above each burner. The Qrave does not.

The design of drain valve struck me as silly. To open the valve, you have to push the handle down. i.e. into the bucket. I bought a 4" long x 3/4" piece of thread pipe and a coupling and attached them to the bottom of the valve. I moved the bucket hook down, so the new coupling serves to keep it in place. I pleased with the results.

I decided to clean the plan. First, I scraped loose stuff and discarded it. Then I filled it to about 3/8" below the top of the pan and added some dish soap.I turned up the heat to high, closed the lid and let it heat the water for 10 minutes. Then I turn off the gas and let the hot water sit for 30 minutes. I got a standard bucket (the one that is provided can't hold this much water) and drained the pan. I have to say that the drain works really well. Fast. I used a scouring pad with some Bar Keepers Friend and water to finish the job. The pan is discolored, but clean. I like this feature.
top_rant.title: This is different that a regular grill

top_rant.text:
UPDATE after three months:

It's not good as an ups. Or backup power when connected to mains. This won't be addressed as there are no firmwares or anything to update.

After a few month's, and a few power outages, and after already realizing it doesn't automatically turn back on, I kept it. However the UPS function has more flaws. It fails under a typical brownout. So any short brownout or surge, may not reliably keep power going. It may turn off. I just don't think it's correct to market any UPS functionality. Yes, it can "work". In a lab maybe. But under real world conditions it does not.

Examples:
Long power outage: AC loss, Battery drains completely, that's fine, but when AC recovers, the inverter will not restore power. So you'd have to be there to turn it back on once it reaches 2% charge.

Medium clean power outage: If AC power is removed, the anker will carry the load, and when AC cleanly recovers, it'll passthrough and charge. My computers handle this fine. This is the only scenario that works how an ups should work.

Brownouts: Apparently under these conditions, there is no hysteresis, and the anker will just turn off. If power comes back on too quickly?, it'll turn off. A brownout happened while you were gone? Better make sure your fridge isn't off. You had a brownout while sleeping? I guess your CPAP will just stay off because the power blipped :/ So for my real world conditions, it's just as likely to interrupt power than provide it. I've just never had issues like this, with any ups I've ever owned.

Execution is 90%, and anker agreed to take it back for a 90% refund after 3 months. I dunno.
=====================
Initial review

It's very expensive.
The manual is incomplete.
Lot of sales/marketing cruft can be misleading.
Anker support is pretty responsive. They don't get sick of answering questions. They comprehend them and generally give complete answers.
I have faith in their 5 year warranty.
It's a good product.

I don't need a device like this all the time. But I figured it could be a decent computer UPS when not needed elsewhere. It's a very powerful UPS but falls short in a couple areas.

UPS stuff:
1. It does not restore power like a normal ups can. This means if you run it down completely, once AC is restored, you need to press the on button again :/ I wouldn't trust it to run a freezer, without human intervention/monitoring, and in fact, I wouldn't leave it inline all the time, in case it got turned off.
2. It does not present any warnings before turning off. I don't think there is anything that can beep on the device. It powers off at 1%, and won't allow you to power it back on (passthrough) until 2% (which it reaches quickly). The display will be off because the timeout is short.
3. It seems more likely to accidentally NOT run your load. If it trips, it won't try again. If it dies, it'll not be on anymore, until you turn it on. It should always be on when AC is connected, and you just cannot be guaranteed that it'll be inverting when the is AC power available. Perhaps I'm being too hard, but no, I wouldn't keep something I wasn't monitoring, connected full time, whereas I WOULD keep my old APC ups inline. Those are just facts.

Something I didn't find in the manual, or the marketing crap.... is how to calculate inverter run time.
Device run time = 1229Wh*0.8/(<device watts> +30W). So 80% efficient, and 30w consumed in non eco mode, if the inverter is on. This is all reasonable.

Run times:
The inverter uses 30w (on battery) just to be "on". This means the longest it can run with no load is around 48-50 hours. Just keep that in mind. Don't run small loads on the inverter. Run them on DC. There you are limited to 120w + whatever usb can do (which is a lot).

So if you are running a 12w load
That's 23.4 hours on inverter.
inverter runtime = 1229Wh*0.8/(12w +30W)

If you run it on DC
That's 80 hours'ish on DC
dc runtime = 1229Wh*0.9/ 12W+(1~2W)

So if you need a fan, probably get a dc fan. Same with lights. Same with most light loads, because you'll be limited otherwise and it's not intuitive, if you are basing things on their marketing/cruff. It's not lies, it's just, I dislike marketing crap on websites that give vague statements that are partly true.

If you are already having to run a load on the inverter, then running smaller loads in addition, will affect runtime less. Perhaps this is common knowledge to people, but I just wanted to drive it home. The marketing page might have you believe you can run your 12w lightbulb for 80 hours though..... That's partly true, just not on AC.

DC input.
The dc input is limited to 30v and also 10amps?. This is a confusing and somewhat limiting voltage. It's right on the cusp of a 60cell solar panel. Technically, you can charge on a 60cell panel, but you may have to shade it, or wait for clouds, to bring the open voltage low enough, to start charging, then the current will bring the voltage down. The good news is, it doesn't explode seeing 40v on the input, but it also doesn't allow any current to flow. So for solar, It's basically a mess. I'm still not certain how the DC input is "supposed" to function. There are no useful docs about it's behavior.

For me, that meant a 240w 60cell panel, once coerced below 30v, and charging, did around 170w at best. YMMV.

Now lets talk about 12v charging. It'll only pull 120w. This makes sense as perhaps it's a car, and it shouldn't just melt your wires :) So it appears to me, that the DC input, is current limited to 10amps.

Basically, the DC input, is both confusing, and less useful. But hey, it seems to protect itself from overvoltage, and CAN charge from solar. But there is nothing specific in the docs about how it actually functions, and I'm completely guessing only under ideal conditions, of 30v, will it ever actually do 300w.....
I assume the panels they sell, in reality are 12v panels, in parallel, which will still only yield 120w best case. But may still yield 120w under not ideal conditions.... But I don't actually know, because there are no docs on this stuff. Anker, wasn't so forthcoming about the DC input, other than it's ok to hook up a 60cell panel, and "it just does not work when the voltage is over 30V." My guess is 10 amps limited.

I have not viewed sign waves, or tested for interference....

The AC charger/inverter is fast. It makes up for all the things I don't like. It's the right size, the right build quality, and has enough power.

The reasons I kept this expensive device.
1. I think anker will stand behind it, for 5 years. They have chat, and email support. I've talked to both.
2. It charges exceptionally fast. 1000w is no joke. It's great for someone like me, that only remembers I need it, in an hour from now :) It's just convenient. It also cannot be throttled, but I've yet to pop a circuit breaker.
3. It's quiet. Save for the inverter getting a little "ringy" running a 1450w microwave, the fans and inverter are quiet. It's quiet. It really stays pretty quiet :)
4. It's the biggest I want to carry, or be mobile with. It's the right size and still moveable :)
5. usb charges things at 90w no problem. It's pretty legit.
6. Even though the ups stuff is flawed, it can back two workstations and monitors, and doesn't break a sweat running 900w+ on battery. That far exceeds my 1400va ups, which would start screaming at 700w. It's also more predictable displaying the projected runtime, so even though it doesn't yell, I can tell when I need to turn off my computers if I remember to look and click the display button......

Other things I don't like:
1. it's actually really hard to see the sockets :) It sounds dumb, but in the dark it's hard to see that stuff.
2. It's not a real ups :/ It's so close that it makes me a little upset with anker. They shouldn't call it an USP.
3. I've pressed the inverter button many times by accident, when attempting to press the display button :/ It's so bad that I'm going to put a cover on that inverter button. I'd have preferred a switch!
4. The display timeout is not adjustable, and is usually way too short. See number 3.
5. The dc input..... It's limited.
6. The lack of a high powered dc output.

That's my review. And I kept it, but unfortunately, I'm still not done testing it. Which is a testament to the lack of technical information available.
top_rant.title: I kept it, despite some flaws, now I regret my use case (UPS) and the money

top_rant.text:
UPDATE 06/11/2017: The hot tub is officially trash! I patched an air leak on the outside (on a seam) no problem. Now the water is leaking out of the plumbing for the 4 high powered jets! This plumbing is inside the liner where it's filled with air, so you can't access the plumbing to attempt any sort of fix. There's also black flakes blowing out of heated / filtered water output into the tub. This happens even though it was just refilled and had new filters installed. There's many reviews that mention the same thing, so I'm not the only one! It was nice for a year while it worked correctly, but these are not built to last! Would NOT RECOMMEND this product, do yourself a favor and finance a real quality hot tub! Look at my attached image to see where the water level leaks down to and the black flakes in the water.

UPDATE 03/16/2016: After 8 months of use the saltwater chlorine generator has stopped working! The control unit would beep and flash error code "E91", which the LED code chart indicates low salt levels. I used salt test strips to raise the salt level back to the ideal 2500ppm, but the error code would not go away. After following all troubleshooting suggestions in the user manual (remove & clean the electrolytic cell and ensure cell cord is plugged in firmly), I was left with calling Intex to file a warranty claim. I was on the phone with a representative within 5 minutes, and although she was hard to understand, patience paid off and she got my warranty claim started. I ended up calling back because I didn't receive the email to complete the claim, they ended up not getting my email correct the first time. I stress the point that you must speak very clear and spell things out for them, and if two letters sound alike you need to say a word with it so they get the right letter (M as in Mike, or N as in Nancy). Yes it would be nice to speak with someone with fluent English, but don't expect it. Nonetheless they did get me a new control unit shipped out ASAP after I completed the warranty claim online. They do not have specific replacement parts to send you, even though the website and user manual list spare part numbers! This is why they sent me a whole new control unit instead of just sending me a new electrolytic cell. It isn't a big deal now, but after my warranty is expired I won't be happy having to buy a whole new control unit if all I need is a single part! Should this issue happen again outside of my warranty, I will just start sanitizing it manually with a floater since the rest of the hot tub functions fine (heater, bubbles, and jets). I'm going to call back and ask if I get a new 1 year warranty on this replacement control unit, because that seems reasonable to me. I don't think they will though, so if this happens again in 8 months I'm SOL! I always kept the water chemistry correctly balanced and took the best care of this hot tub, so it's a shame that something broke after 8 months! I hope the replacement lasts much longer! (Minus 1 star rating for this ordeal)

Original Review:

I bought the Jet & Bubble Deluxe Massage Spa Set. I highly recommend this model since it includes 4 high powered water jets in addition to the standard 120 air bubbles! This is the closest you will get to a true hot tub with an inflatable. I researched all inflatable hot tubs and this one is the best by far!

This hot tub is a major upgrade from the bubble only versions! I loved my first one, but this one is so much better! Below I describe all the Pros and Cons.

PROS:
- JETS: The 0.9 HP pump produces 4 high pressure water jets that can be manually angled and adjusted for varying water pressures (jets and bubbles can be operated separately). You can shut off 1, 2, or even 3 jets to divert more pressure to one jet which is awesome! They are plenty strong enough. Better than the bubbles in my opinion, heat stays in the tub longer. If they ever make a newer model I'd love to see maybe 8 or even 16 jet models. Although then the heat wouldn't stay on with the jets because of the more powerful pump so it's a trade off really when running off a normal outlet.
- BUBBLES: The 1.1 HP blower injects 120 warmed air bubble jets surrounding the perimeter of the hot tub (NOTE: Bubbles will cool down the hot tub water quicker than the jets, since the bubbles are forcing cooler outside air into the tub. Even though the air is warmed, cooler nights will cause the air entering the tub to still be cold)
- HEAT: The 1300 watt heater has two stages and can keep one stage on even while the jets or bubbles are going!!! This is a huge plus over the bubble only version, which shut off the heater when the bubbles were activated. This way you can stay in longer on cooler nights without the water temperature dropping so quick! (NOTE: The heater goes on standby when both the jets and bubbles are running together. The heater can only stay on with either the jets or bubbles running separately, not with both.) Heater is capable of raising the water temperature 2-3ºF/hr (1-1.5ºC/hr)
- SALTWATER SYSTEM: This is built into the control unit, and has a specific button to operate and set the daily running hours (3hr, 5hr, 8hr). Saltwater chlorinaters use a small amount of 99.99% pure salt that's added to the hot tub at startup to produce natural chlorine. This process sanitizes the hot tub water, removing the need for any dangerous packaged chemicals. Salt is very cheap at your local pool store compared to buying chemicals all the time! The best part is, you only need to add salt once at startup! Salt does not evaporate, and is only lost when water is removed from the hot tub through draining, overflow, or splashing. Most times you will only need to top off the water level and the salt level will still be in an acceptable range! This makes balancing chemicals and overall spa maintenance so simple, as long as the electrolytic cell is turned on and operated properly DAILY. This system must be manually turned on daily in order to maintain proper chlorine levels in the hot tub. I run mine 3-8 hours a night, depending on usage. The user manual has instructions to remove, inspect, and clean the electrolytic cell (should be inspected frequently).
- HARD WATER TREATMENT SYSTEM: This operates in conjunction with the water filter (should run the filter 24/7 & clean the filters weekly). It makes the water gentler on skin, clothes, and the entire spa system. This is an upgrade over the other models, and will help make sure you have years of trouble free spa use!
- FILTER PUMP: The filter pump uses 2 cartridges at a time that are inserted into housings and screwed onto the inside of the hot tub. They look like they'd get in the way, but they don't. This is good because when one becomes dirty, there's a second one for water to filter through. These filters seem to be made better than the ones my old inflatable hot tub used, with more fins to catch debris and thicker filter paper. The filter pump is slow, so it won't filter out heavy debris that sinks to the bottom of the hot tub. I use a fine net to skim the bottom and top of the hot tub before entering. Besides this, the filter does a great job at keeping the spa water clean and clear! The filter is rated at 460 gal/hr (That means the whole hot tub is filtered about every 30 minutes). Make sure to clean the filters once a week or more if heavily used! Replace monthly.
- QUIET: The control unit and pumps seem to run pretty quiet compared to my old inflatable hot tub. The air bubbles sound like a shop vac while running and are the loudest part. The jets are a mild humming while running, quieter than the bubbles for sure. The filter (which I leave running 24/7) is a low humming noise. The saltwater system and hard water treatment system are both silent. The buttons make an audible beeping noise whenever pressed, kind of like a microwave noise level.
- STURDY: You can sit on the side of the hot tub if you only want to dip your feet in or get dizzy while sitting in the hot tub. The tub is well constructed and will last you a long time if setup properly. Make sure there's no sharp objects on the ground where the hot tub is setup at. Make sure the hot tub is not exposed to direct sunlight all the time. Protect the hot tub and the control unit from rain and severe weather if possible. All these tips will help make sure your liner lasts for many years!

CONS:
- COST TO OPERATE: $30/month during the summer, and $60/month during the winter (indoors in basement). It's worth it in my opinion if you use the hot tub enough! (Measured using a Wemo Insight Switch)
- CAN'T SANITIZE & HEAT AT THE SAME TIME: When the saltwater sanitizer is operating, the heater goes into standby until sanitation is complete. You can't heat and sanitize at the same time. You will need to have the saltwater system operating daily at a time when you know you'll never need it at max temperature. Remember that the hot tub will lose a couple degrees during this time period and will require a couple more hours after sanitation is complete to reach your set temperature again. If the flame icon is ever flashing green, this indicates the heater is on standby.
- SMALL FILTER SYSTEM: The small filters get dirty pretty quick and definitely need cleaned every week at least (more often if heavily used). The water flow in the filter system is very low pressure to allow for heating. You should keep the filter pump running 24/7 because of how low the flow is. It does the job, don't get me wrong, it's just a very slow flow of water. (Water jets do not go through the filters like I thought they would, they have a separate suction outlets behind the 2 filter housings. They say to remove the filters and housings when using the jets, but I can tell it doesn't affect the pressure of the jets much at all if you leave them in.)

Overall, I think this product is a fine piece of engineering! I would recommend this to anyone seeking a cheap alternative to a true spa (jets, bubbles, heat, saltwater sanitation, hard water treatment). If you maintain the water chemistry properly and keep the tub itself and control unit protected from extreme sunlight and weather, you will have years of trouble free use! I will periodically update this review as needed.

UPDATE 9/26/15: Having owned this model for just about 2 months now, I can say it is holding up great so far! The only noticeable issue is some yellow staining on the white vinyl wherever the water has been touching. I bought some spa stain enzyme to try and help prevent this in the future. Highly suggest hooking up a WeMo Insight device to track the power usage so you aren't shocked when you see your electric bill! I still love and use mine 3 - 5 times a week, so satisfying and relaxing!

UPDATE 1/26/16: I am now 6 months in and having no issues at all! The yellow staining mentioned in the previous update has not worsened at all and I now barely notice it. Water stays clean with the filter system running 24/7 and I make a set of filters last a month (clean weekly). I run the sanitizer 3-8 hrs a night (depending on usage) to maintain proper chlorine levels. Use test strips or a test kit to properly maintain your water chemistry, failure to do so will damage the hot tub equipment and liner over time! The water seems to stay fresh for up to 3 months if you are pretty clean before entering (ideally anyone using it should shower prior). Eventually the water will get a slimy feel to it and you will know it's about time to change the water. I didn't have the best experience draining this thing, it leaked everywhere! The hose had a rubber gasket, but it wasn't sealing for some reason to the tub's drain. Half the water went through the hose, and the other half leaked out on the deck. This will pose a small issue for me with it being in my basement during the winter. I might have to buy a small electric pump to drain this thing if I can't get it to drain without any leaks.

UPDATE 2/2/16: I had a small leak at connection "B" until I hand tightened it even more. I left a dry paper towel under it and it's stayed dry so far after snugging it up.
top_rant.title: Not Built To Last, Even When Taken Care Of!

top_rant.text:
[[VIDEOID:1fcec3335eb435b3cb28fc085ca70edc]] Update 9 Dec 16 - Well, got another summer out of it but not without lots of difficulty. Spent the whole summer fighting the dread E90 (low water flow) error. Usually this was indicative of clogged filters but then I was getting it every day or two even with a brand new set of filters. By the end of the summer started getting an indication of the real problem - small pools of water under the filter pointed toward a leak somewhere in the filter system. This was confirmed by bleeding the air from the pump frequently, which kept the filter running longer. Still as time wore on, it got to the point where the filter would not stay running but a few hours (or even minutes!).

Emptied the tub and tore through everything that was readily accessible and couldn't find the leak. Put everything back together and filled the tub back up and things got worse. Emptied the tub again. This time I pulled the intake pump assembly and found what I thought was the culprit - a piece of cheap silicone tubing between the pump outlet and the inlet to the heater. It was highly deteriorated, and I hardly think this material was a good design choice for a high-chlorine environment like a hot tub.

Now the real fun began - trying to find a replacement. Intex was totally useless in this regard. I searched all over looking for something to substitute, but the Chinese design was metric-sized tubing. Finally I settled on a piece of 1" automotive heater hose. It's ID was too small and I had to whittle out the insides of the hose to get it to fit over the pump outlet and compression fitting for the union to the heater inlet. It took hours of..ah hem...creative engineering, but I finally got everything all together again.

Fill the tub back up, start heating up, and next morning there's water all over the patio. The union washer failed going to the heater inlet. Great. Tear it all apart again. Once again, no replacement from Intex. Once again, engineer a new washer from one I could find locally. Re-assemble the whole mess again, and start the heating process.

Within an hour it was apparent there was a much bigger leak. Now the inlet union between the tub itself and the filter inlet was leaking at a furious pace. All the disassembly of the tub from the filter tore the union washer. Back to the Intex site and LO! they actually show the washer and have them in stock!! Joy Joy! Then I tried to order it. The web site is hosed up. After a bazillion attempts, it refused to confirm any of 3 known good credit cards for the purchase. Left e-mail for Intex asking how I could purchase the washer. Of course there was NO RESPONSE. I ended up buying a piece of rubber floor molding at Lowe's that was the correct width and cutting several washers out of it before I finally got one that would stop the leakage.

Got about 3 weeks of trouble free tubbing. But then I started finding the filter controls completely shut off. The GFCI in the cord had gone bad and refused to reset using the pushbutton. The controls would only reset if I pulled the cord entirely out of the outlet for 10-15 seconds. But at least the tub would stay running for a few days, until the next dread E90 would shut it down again. With no reset at the GFCI there was no recourse other than to pull the plug again.

A couple weeks of this foolishness, and finally the controls were shutting down again completely. But now after cycling the power cord, they would only stay on for a couple seconds, a minute at most, before dying again. I found that this was killing the GFCI for the circuit in the garage, not the one in the cord. After a hour of playing around I found the filter controls stayed up, as long as I left the heater off. Great. Someone tell me what good is a hot tub without a heater?

I've had it. This thing has turned out to be a completely cheap Chinese piece of junk from a complete useless cheap Chinese piece of company. If you are foolish enough to order one of these, please don't say the you weren't warned!!!

Update 28 Apr 16 - after waiting a week for Fedex to get the replacement tub across the country, it arrived. On opening the box I was in for a rude shock. It looks like the wrong tub!! SH!T! My pump has 4 connectors (A-D) but the tub has 3 (A-C). I immediately jump on the phone and call Intex back. Once again I get another Chinese lady that speaks English, but with such poor pronunciation I barely understand her at all. I literally spent 15 minutes to determine that the TUB they sent was octogon shaped, because I thought she was saying TOP. It was so bad I had to insist that I get a representative that spoke better English. This woman argued with me and insisted her English was fine. I asked to speak to a supervisor. She flatly refused. I was absolutely furious at this horrendous customer service!

I spent an hour on the phone only to find out that, technically, they had sent the correct tub - for the 2016 model. The tub has been "upgraded' from the 2015 model. This new tub no longer supports the sanitation system. So let me get this straight. Intex...You "upgrade" your product by removing features? WTF!!

I was instructed to attach the 2016 tub to my 2015 controls and leave the D connection on the pump opened and unplugged. Intex has no means to plug this unused connection and the rep pretty much told me to piss off and deal with it on my own. More stellar customer service.

One final thing is that this replacement tub is smaller than the original. It measured 77"x77" OD, and the new one is only 72"x72". I'm 6'1" and I could leisurely stretch out in the original tub. Now my legs are just a touch too long, and I have to bend my knees or run my feet up the side to get myself to fit. Where the old tub held about 225g, the new one holds about 10% less at 200g. Less is more...profits that is. Looks like they changed the material as well. I doubt it is even as good as the material that failed on the original tub,

I only give this 2 stars now because for at least 10 months I was satisfied with product too bad the manufacturers are total idiots.

Update 18 Apr 16 - Whacked a star off, but it might have been a couple more. Ten months in one of the inner panels of the tub de-laminated, creating a huge bubble that was leaking air allowing the tub to deflate. The short video shows the problem clearly.

Called Intex Customer Service seeking warranty replacement. There is no online, e-mail or chat options. You must call the toll free number once you can find it on their site. As others have noted, finding a representative that speaks passable English is a challenge. Once past the language barrier I received an e-mail with a link to a warranty request form. After some jumping around through hoops for several days, I finally received an e-mail today saying my request was approved and the new tub will be shipped in 2-3 days, I will update again when it arrives.

Now that I've had this for a couple weeks, and have used it frequently, I have been pleasantly surprised. I own a regular hot tub at my legal residence, but I am renting a house in the town I am working in 300 miles away, so I needed something portable. I did quite a bit of researching before I settled on this Intex model. It comes in 4 large boxes, two of which are heavy enough you will want to get some help lifting. I was impressed with the material used on this tub. Some of the ones I saw at other retailers used a single heavy layer of vinyl, like you would get with an above-ground pool. This is made more like an inflatable boat, with multiple layers of vinyl and what I believe is Hypalon. It was very easy to set up, once I actually read the instructions <DOH!>.Assembling the controls to the pump was a snap, and having the inflator built right into the pump mechanism was a great idea. The tub inflated in maybe 10 minutes. At a 220g. capacity, I had it filled and heating up in less than two hours. It did take a while to get up to temperature. The fill water was about 80F and it took about 15 hours to get to 102F, on a fairly warm and humid evening. I'm 6'1" and had no issues getting in or out of the tub, but folks with shorter legs are going to want some steps to enter and exit. The tub was the perfect size for me, and I was easily able to stretch out to enjoy the pulsations of the jets and the bubble outlets. While this is listed as a 4 person tub, I would say that only 2 fully grown adults would be very comfortable in the space. Mom, Dad and smaller children might be OK, but 4 adults will need some help untangling their legs after a session in the tub. One thing I noticed that might be a caveat for some is that the tub loses temperature very rapidly with the cover off and the jets on. The heater is turned off if you use the jets and in the half-hour session the controls automatically allow I regularly drop from 102F down to 98F. It doesn't other me at all, but others might not be so forgiving. I thought the floating cover was a clever idea, and the snap on straps do hold it on well, as we had so heavy thunderstorm winds blow through, but the cover was well secured. With the cover on, the tub does heat much more quickly and it seems to hold the temperature steady on the mild evenings we've had in our area lately. So while this is not quite as plush, or hold temperature as well as a full-blown hot tub, it certainly is an excellent alternative for those that do not have the location or the funds.
top_rant.title: CAVEAT EMPTOR!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

top_rant.text:
Ok so I'm middle aged and I ran away screaming from Chemistry 101 years ago. But yet, being in Florida, I became possessed with the idea to get an AG (above ground) pool for the summer. Well I got one, an Intex 15 x 48 (review coming soon) I set it up, filled it up and then I went to the pool store, I went to WallyWorld (word to the wise DO NOT ASK A WALLY WORLD EMP. any pool questions, they know as much as I do!), I went to the OTHER pool store, then back to the first pool store. I had water tested, more water tested, I added bags of shock (which FYI you are not only not supposed to NOT breath,(hello-it's a powder!) NOT get on your skin, but they can EXPLODE!? (mind you this is setting it up, no one (me) had been in it and its not like I have 5 kids running around-I don't even own 1) a jug o' scale slime reducer and ph down and some kinda acid that sounds like Cyanide?!?! (that I had to put in a sock and tie to the ladder so it wouldn't harm the vinyl-and I'm going to put my SKIN in there)?? and even got one of those handy dandy nifty cool sharkey floating chlorinators and while putting the tablets in I tried to back away from the smell-which is almost impossible. For my multiple purchases and over [...] worth of chemicals I got a free 4 inch beach ball that I got to watch float around with the stinky sharky chlorinator in a pool that I couldn't use! WOOO HOOOO! Every time I was in the pool store I found myself backing away toward the door, hand over mouth, going,"how do you guys work in here, it STINKS!" but still somehow they'd get my money and Id get bag full of "THE RIGHT" chemicals.

With all the guidance and chemistry I managed to change my water from clear, to milky white, milky brown, brownish brown and greenish brown. My hands, despite the gloves (that leaked) and gas mask, became dry, sore, raw and broke out in some weird red itchy painful bumps. Finally having enough I drained that sucker, figuring Id kill some major plant life. (My plants are now bigger than ever? Go figure? :-/ While draining it...it was lucky I didn't slip n fall as it was that kind slimy, in less than 2 weeks time and despite the chems.

Still wanting a pool and after much reading...in desperation, I ordered this pump/generator and went without groceries for a week. OMG I could hug it and cry, I love it that much!
You fill up your pool.
You go to wallyworld and buy pool salt (and maybe some PH down)
You put 100-150lb in your pool (about [...] per 40lbs at Wallyworld)
You turn on the THE VERY VERY POWERFUL 2000gph FILTER pump for 24 hours (unlike the rinky dink junk that comes with the pools-520gph-mean you need to run it 10 hour a day at least for a 15 by 48 when NOT in USE!)
The next day you turn on the generator.
And you have a CHLORINATED, chemical free (except for the ph down) wonderful feeling, CLEAN, CLEAR pool!
Now I've only had it running 2 weeks, and its still too cool to get in it, but the test strips test fine, tho my ph remains a little high and I'm using VERY HARD straight from the ground deep well water, and I could get in it any time if it was warmer. My hands aren't breaking out or cracking and there is NO chlorine odor. Its hard to explain but the water "feels different" in a good way!

Now before I bought this I read a lot of whining about programming it, that kinda concerned me. I'm NOT techie! Now that I own it...its so easy its laughable. Beep, beep beep, beep beep, Beeeeep, beeeep and you are DONE and I'm serious as long as it took me to type that, thats how fast it programs. Takes me longer to et an alarm clock!! And you can run just the pump if you need to without the generator. I'm serious. So who cares if the power is interrupted or you want to run it at a different time. The first time I ran it (and the second) it started beebing insanely at me (beep beep beep beep beep) and flashing its little red light till it got my attention-its showed a 91 (low salt) off I went to get more salt and its been happy (93) ever since. Its pretty much idiot proof. Also it was VERY easy to hook up and is VERY quiet.

My Only complaints/concerns thus far-
The outside connections are a bit heavy on the side of the pool-make sure you have everything tight.
And unlike just the Intex pump, that tells you to unplug it before you get in the pool, this one says to leave it plugged in. Just common sense and safety says to unplug it even with a GFC plug-its NOT hard to reprogram!
For the 15 by 48 the manual tells you 100lbs of salt and 3 hours run time.
Reality is its 140lbs of salt and 4 hours run time.

I am FINALLY looking forward to being a pool owner and this summer! This generator saved my sanity, and I think it is SOOO much healthier than ALL THOSE CHEMICALS (just read the packages, they should scare the ....outta you-and then you are going to dip your body in them?? After it practically tells you to use gloves to the elbow and a gas mask and to utilize them. NO THANK YOU-BYE BYE pool store, BYE BYE evil chemicals, by bye cool sharky floater thingy. If anything changes, or my water color changes I will update, but for the time being...I LOVE THIS THING, well worth the money!!!

UPDATE...It is now the end of May. I sold my cool sharkey floater LOL. I live in FL and it has BEEN HOT-all days 90-95 degrees! Now my pool is shaded for half of the day but in return I get leaves and oak pollen on the bottom which is next to impossible to get up. Still this pump is doing a FANTASTIC JOB! My water has been sparkly clear since I fired her up! Its now warm enough that Ive been swimming and the water is very gentle to your skin. I have very hard water and Ive had to ad NO OTHER CHEMICALS which for me is a JOY as I HATE chemicals. (I should prob ad some PH down but WHY, that stuff is an evil acid and my pool feels and looks great.
The only thing I can say is that every time it rains I do get a low salt reading and do have to ad about 1/2 a bag of salt...and in FL where it rains every afternoon, well I guess Ill be going thru a lot of salt. ;) We did have one REALLY BAD rain event and I shut down the pump due to the lightening and forgot to re set it and the next day my pool was lightly milky. I almost paniced. Well I just added a bag of salt, chlorinated for 3 hours and then about 7 hours later I ran it for 4 hours and PROBLEM SOLVED. Clear beautiful water returned! The Intext test strips DO NOT WORK in checking your chlorine level...they give you a color that matches NOTHING on the bottle. When I took a pool water sample to the pool store they could register no chlorine????? (I did not tell them it was a salt water pool) and they handed me a list of about $100 worth of chemicals I needed to buy and I laughed and left empty handed. So if there is an issue its that I cannot check the Chlorine ppm on a intex test strip or at a pool store-would love suggestions on this as well as a NATURAL chemical free way to lower the PH. And I know something is working great as I live on a farm and every water trough-even those in the shade, bleached, scrubbed clean and refilled will turn nasty green in 24-48 hours. My Pool...CRYSTAL CLEAR! ANd now that Ive been in it, no slime!!! I STILL love this pump and HIGHLY RECOMMEND IT esp for pool newbies like me! So simple and results are fantastic! And for anyone who HATES chemicals as badly as I do and who has senstivie middle aged skin like I do, for any size pool this is the only way to go-a salt water system. I sound like a commercial but after doing it the old fashioned way the first time, I could not be more thrilled with this generator!
PS I did get a low flow reading one day..another moment of panic...I just pulled out the filter, hosed it, drained the filter compartment (the turny knob on the bottom that looks just like the one on the top), and walah, all good as new again! If you hose your filter and not doing a heavy load in the pool you can get them to last about 3 weeks and I can tell you after shopping around AMAZON is the CHEAPEST place to buy your B filters for this pump-get them in the case of 6 and even with shipping you save BIG $$ compared to local stores.
top_rant.title: OMG Why Would You Live Without One for your Pool?

top_rant.text:
Yep.... 2nd attempt at hoping.
... and hoping and hoping and hoping....

Perhaps it's past 'birthing' season?
Mind you though we've had decent summer weather (nothing to write home about, but at least 4-7 days of good summer temps). But these guys are supposed to enjoy 'decent temps'..... SO WHERE THE 'blank' ARE THEY THEN???!?!?!!!

I'm now a bad gift-giver twice over on the same gift but from 2 different sellers at this point!!!! I will say one thing... never, never buy from Hirt's Gardens. Only a small percentage there has had luck with their purchase (at any time of the year). If you're giving it a try, and only due to reviews here -- I say buy here, not there.

I really am starting to think that these folk sell stuff waaaay beyond the 'season to sell' stuff ----- OR, it's a hope and a prayer as to the outcome of said 'egg sacs' when it comes to any buyer at any time!!!!!!

I had a 'godly' moment with a PM 1 year ago....
..... in the midst of a 113 degree heatwave here in LA, I had bumped into this poor Praying Mantis barely alive sitting on the wall that I like to sit on and people-watch during my breaks.

Due to my 'animal-loving instincts', I proceeded to offer the poor thing a bit of my iced coffee just to help quell any poss upcoming death due to heat. Boy howdy, was he thankful. He sat and drank that bit-o-drip-o-coffee up like he hadn't drank anything in a long time :)

Seeing his distress and how he somehow managed to look me straight in the eye and ask for more.... work became a frivilous moment. I ran back inside and got a full cup of real water for the poor thing to drink. I politely poured it around him and egaaaads.... he took off sucking the water down like there was no tomorrow.

At that point, we had bonded... you could see it in his eyes ;)

And, at that point... work become nothing but caring for this poor little creature. Mind you, I had never seen a PM prior in my life and was completely flabbergasted at how this thing took to me ---- let alone looked at me and watched me.

My day was filled with nothing but caring for this poor distraught little insect (thank god the main mgr wasn't in that day). Every 15 min or so I'd go out and make sure he was okay, or give him more water, or just sit with him. It really was wierd and fabulous all at the same time because he seemed to just know that I was there to help him.

He definitely showed a 'perked-up-ness' up by midday and continued to drink all the water I offered him throughout the rest of the afternoon. Come 6 pm, it was time for me to go home and having to pass him in order to get to the elevator to my car ---- I wished him 'godspeed' :) Take care and be good little bug :) Hope I helped you little bug :)

So.... day turns into night and night turns into morning and I'm due back at work at 9am. I buzz the buzzer and as the door opens.... a coworker blats out to me about my friend the praying mantis who followed me into the store overnight.

"He's in the trash now" she said.
I said.... what!???!?!?!?!
Please back up!!!!!!!

She, upon 'opening the store' had thought that she'd seen a branch of leaves that had blown into the store overnight. She proceeded to grab the leaves and branch and, lo-and-behold..... it wasn't leaves or a branch.... it was my praying mantis friend!!!!

She admitted sadly sort of crushing him in her 'debris pick-up' and now he was 'crushed' and suffering in the trashcan :(
She had given him water in the hopes of helping him but in essence drowned him with the over-offering of water in the trashcan.

I ran to the trashcan and found my little friend and immediately pulled him out of his demise. I feared too late a save, but still worthy of the attempt. I then took him back outside and placed him amongst the geraniums flowerbed that existed beside our store. He didn't look too well, but I could hope.

All in all, he didn't make it. Holly had crushed him, killed him (by accident mind you). She was just as startled as you or I might have been to pick up a branch of leaves and have them not be leaves. The poor little thing never had a chance after her :(

I had checked in on the poor little guy throughout the morning but as things looked worse and worse, but I finally had to stop checking (for my own peace of mind). A year later however.... those geraniums are looking beyond fab today! His little soul gave life to some abFab flowers! :)

It appears that the darned little thing had followed my scent into the retail store where I worked. He then proceeded to squeeze underneath the glass locked door (front door), and then crawled up the entrance path and settled in at the front of the store.

Now mind you, this little fellow had plenty of options of 'where to go' once the heat subsided a bit in the evening. But no, he came up the drive, made a left, went straight forward for about 15 feet, then made a left, and then squeezed underneath a sliding closed door, then made another left once inside, moved approx 10-15 up a ramp, and then made a right and proceeded to settle in. Perhaps he would have gone further, perhaps not. My guess is that he had a nice cool place to stay the rest of the night. Come morning, perhaps he would have moved along further into the store, perhaps not.... but by then Holly the Bug Killer had made her way to the front of the store doing her opening duties. Both he and she met in still what I consider, a very interesting place since there was no reason for this praying mantis to even be inside the store. He could have turned right at the top of the driveway but didn't. Had he turned right, he would have found all the cheese he could eat @ the BH Cheese Store. He also could have continued on after turning left towards our entrance on the sidewalk, but didn't.

This little thing actually seemed to smell his way towards me from the day prior of walking in and out and making sure he was okay. Extremely weird, yet very touching. And should you take the time to read about these things... they really are fabulous. And it would appear that he and I found each other right during the high-times of mating. Really, read up on these little mongers. They've sight and smell beyond anything known... and the males will do anything for a mate --even if dying upon the way.

Friendly little things I tell ya. I shall never forget my little friend, and shall always remember him as a 'special moment' in time :)
So....'the short-long' of it all, hence my 'let's buy a few for Mike & Nick's garden!
Welp..... as of 9/12/2011, they've still not seen a thing anywhere in their garden :(

Perhaps I'll try again next spring (earlier than this purchase) or perhaps not. But I shall never forget my day with the praying mantis that deemed me worthy of he following my scent into a closed store at night and then suffering his fate at the hands of 'Holly' :(

At least I know I made a difference for at least a day with a little creature that seemingly called me his friend for a day :)
Thanks little buggie guy, godspeed :)

In retrospect of the above story, my hope for a nest-of-newborns, and the lack of anything evolving from said nest..... I give this seller 2 stars.
One star for the hope of what was to happen, one star for the joy of enjoying what was to happen, and one star thinking I should have perhaps bought earlier in the season? Okay.... perhaps only 2 stars would be appropriate since none of the above happened. And since many here seemed to have a good experience with this seller - I say, give it a shot.... in hopes of possibly finding a 'new friend' next year... and with that ---- 2 stars it shall be.

top_screaming.text:

IT IS VERY LIGHTWEIGHT EASY TO USE AND A PERFECT REPLACEMENT FOR A GAS POWERED ONE, I M GIVING FIVE STARS BECAUSE IT DOESN'T DESERVE TO GET LESS BECAUSE OF THE CONS THAT IT HAS. NO MAJOR ISSUES. IF PEOPLE GIVE IT LESS THAN 5 STARS IT IS BECAUSE THEY ARE NOT USING IT ON WHAT I WAS MADE FOR.....SMALL LAWNS......

AS YOU CAN SEE ON MY PICTURES MY LAWN WAS VERY TALL IT WAS MORE THAN 12 INCHES, BUT THE MOWER DID A GOOD JOB AND DID NOT WEAKENED AT ANY POINT. BATTERY DOES NOT LAST AS LONG AS IT SAYS BUT HEY IT IS WAY ABOVE AVERAGE IT LASTED MORE THAN I WAS EXPECTING IT. I WAS ONLY EXPECTING IT TO RUN FOR 10 MINUTES ON EACH BATTERY.

I WAS ALSO EXPECTING IT TO EAT A LOT OF THE LINE CUTTER BUT IT DIDN'T YOU HAVE TO KEEP IN MIND THAT ON A GAS PWOERED YOU HAVE TO SPEND ON OIL, GAS MAINTENANCE AND HOPE THAT NOTHING HITS THE BALDES BECAUSE AS SOON AS THEY ARE TORN YOU ARE OUT OF A MOWER. SO I SET THESE P4s UP IN A ROOM THAT HAD A ON GOING GROW. AT THE TIME I ONLY HAD 4 ITEMS IN PRODUCTION, SO I PUT 2 ITEMS UNDER 1, P4 AND 2 ITEMS UNDER 1, P150. I THEN GO HOME AND COME BACK THE NEXT MORNING AND WHAT THE ?. THE P4 IS NOT WORKING. IT CAN'T BE THE TIMER CAUSE THE P150 IS KICKING ASS ( as always ). TURNS OUT TO BE THE LIGHT CORD WAS LOOSE IN THE LIGHT.( which after I used both p4s both of them have a problem of staying connected. and then coming in in the morning 4 different times and the p4s are not working I start getting a little upset and thinking this was a bad investment ). NOW A WEEK LATER. WHAT? THE ITEMS UNDER THE P150s ARE OUT DOING THE ITEMS UNDER THE P4s. NOW I'M THINKING S -- T I JUST BLEW A $ 1000.00. LIKE I STATED ON WANTED TO EXPAND. AND I DID NOT THINK THESE P4s WOULD BE ABLE TO PERFORM THE WAY THEY SHOULD ( for the money ). I JUMPED ON GOOD OLD AMAZON AND HAD TO PURCHASE ANOTHER LED FROM ANOTHER MFG. WHEN I RECEIVED THE NEW LED I MOVE THE 4 ITEMS I HAD IN PRO.RM.#2 TO PRO. RM. #3. THEN IN P.R.#2 I SET UP MY EXPANDTION. WITH MY NEW LED IN THE MIDDLE OF THE 2, P4s TO COVER A 4' X 8' AREA WITH 32 ITEMS IN 5 GAL FAB. BAGS ( that's 1 item per s/ft ). A WEEK LATER 29 OF THE 32 CAME UP ( not bad ). NOW I CAN SEE HOW THESE P4s DO AGAIST MY NEW LED ( which has 3 times more watts for half the price of the p4s but it has only a 10 spectrum and has only 1 on/off switch. I love the dual and trip. on the p150s and p4s. save money and that's what it's all about ). NOW 6 WEEKS LATER. ALL LEDs ARE PERFORMING EXCELENT. ALL THE ITEMS UNDER THE DIFFERENT LEDs ARE EXACTLY THE SAME. ALL THE NODES ARE THE SAME, SURE A FEW MAY BE A LITTLE TALLER BUT OTHER THAN THAT THE SAME. BY THE WAY THE P150s IN P.R.#3 ARE STILL KICKING ASS FLOWERING THE 4 PREVIOUS ITEMS WHICH I HARVEST AT THIS TIME. YUMMY. SO THE RESULTS SO FAR WITH MY NEW LED AGAIST THE P4s. IT'S COSTING ME 4% MORE TO RUN MY NEW LED AGAIST THE P4s ( which cost 500% more than my new led, like I stated before the Ps are way over priced. fair would be 100% to 200% mark up. but also like I stated money is what it's all about. to a point ). SO NOW THE ITEMS IN P.R.#2 ARE IN PHASE TWO (flowering ). AND I WILL TRY AND UPDATE THOSE RESULTS WITH THE 2 DIFFERENT LEDs. YOU CAN TAKE FROM THIS WHAT EVER YOU WHAT. BUT I KNOW ONE THING, I WILL STILL BE BUYING PLATINUM LED GROW LIGHTS ( EVEN THOUGH I WOULD BE ABLE TO EAT FOR A FEW MONTHS ). THANKS PLATINUM FOR A GOOD PRODUCT, IT'S SO HARD TO FIND THAT NOW A DAY'S. ( HEY MAYBE IN THE FUTURE YOU COULD SHOW A LITTLE LOVE.CAUSE I AM ALREADY DESIGNING MY NEXT EXPANDTION ). REALY FOLKS THESE LEDs ARE ONE OF THE BEST IF NOT THE BEST IN THE LONG RUN......SINCERLY MR.T.D.

UPDATE THE UPDATE : I FORGOT THESE ARE THE (ONLY) LIGHTS THAT I HAVE FOUND THAT HAVE THE FULL 12 BAND SPECTRUM. THAT ALONE SHOULD TELL YOU SOMETHING. SINCERLY, MR.D..........................................................................

UPDATE TUE.7/12/16 : I JUST FINISHED HARVESTING THE ABOVE GROW. RESULTS WERE OUTSTANDING. I HAD TO CHANGE THE ABOVE SET UP A LITTLE. THE AREA OF THE GROW NOW IS 3'WX7'LX7'H = 21 S/FT WITH THE 2, P4s AT EACH END AND THE OTHER CO. 1600 WATT LIGHT IN THE MIDDLE. I HAD 5 ROWS OF 2 PLANTS PER ROW. ALONG WITH ROOF RATS AND NORWAY RATS. THE LAST 2 OF WHICH ARE THE ONLY THINGS I WANT TO SEE IN THE TRAPS.
THE KEY TO CATCHING A RAT IS LEAVING THE TRAP alone, AND USING THE RIGHT BAIT. ALONG WITH PROPER SETTING. IT'S A GOOD IDEA TO READ ABOUT WHAT RATS OR N YOUR AREA AND HOW TO TRAP THEM & HEALTH CONCERNS.

THE BEST BAIT IS PEANUT BUTTER, BECAUSE IT STANDS UP TO SITTING FOR LONGER PERIODS OF TIME.
SO IF YOU NEED TO SET OUTSIDE WITH THIS TRAP; ( and if you've got vermin IN the house, you've got them outside) YOU can USE A MILK CRATE WITH PATIO BLOCKS OR BRICKS TO HOLD IT DOWN, OR wrap SOME WIRE Into THE SHAPE OF A BOX or AROUND A LARGE PLUMBING PIPE MAKING A LONG TUBULAR SHAPE TO PUT THE TRAP IN. USE LANDSCAPE STAKES, OR CUT UP CLOTHES HANGERS TO HOLD IT DOWN, BECAUSE THE RAT-COONS, cats dogs and many other animals are now at risk for injury.
WHERE I live the coons, and possums SNAP THESE TRAPS AND FLIP THEM OVER AND STEEL THE BAIT, usually breaking the traps with their first contact. IF A RAT GETS TO IT FIRST THE coons EAT THE RATS. (TOTALLY GROSS) BUT THEY ALSO BREAK THE TRAP. I HADE 6 F THESE AT SAY $5. EACH, BROKEN IN ONE NIGHT AND NO RATS. PRETTY EXPENSIVE VENTURE. THE COONS, SQUIRRELS, AND POSSUMS SNAP OFF THE SmaLL PEICE OF PLASTIC AND RUIN THE TRAP USUALLY WITHIN TWO USES and the trap may need to sit undisturbed for several days before they will even go near them. SET THE TRAPS WITH GLOVES ON. RUB SOME PEANUT BUTTER ON THE GLOVES. RATS AND ALL VERMIN HAVE A STRONG SENSE OF SMELL AND INTEL. IF THEY SMELL YOU THE WILL VOID THE TRAP. WHEN FIRST PLACED IT WIL BE NEW NO MATTER WHAT AND THEY WILL BE CAUTIOUS.
ITS DISCOURAGING, EXPENSIVE AND CAN BE A lot OF ALL HEART BREAK.

SO THIS TRAP HAS WORKED WELL FOR ME, BUT DUE TO EXTENUATING CIRCUMSTANCES ITS ALSO BEEN EXPENSIVE, PAINFUL AND ANNOYING. I CALLED THE "TOM CAT" COMPANY; THEY REALLY DIDN'T CARE. IT WOULD BE SO EASY TO IMPROVE THIS TRAP AND MAKE EVEN MORE MONEY HAD THEY WANTED TO LISTEN TO ME. a woman!

So I use the VICTOR (chick) TRAPS. they are about a 1.50 cheaper and better made. OF COURSE THEY ARE ALL MADE TO END UP BEING NOT REALLY WORTH THE MONEY. CLEAR PLANNED obsolescence. HIRING A RAT GUY IS REALLY EXPENSIVE, AND THEY DON'T DO ANYTHING YOU CAN'T DO. IF YOU'VE GOT A HEAVY INFESTATION HOWVER YOU SHOULD CALL FOR PROFESSIONAL HELP. YOUR BEST BET IS TO TRY TO AVOID ATTRACTING THEM. HOWEVER, IM SURE THERE IS SOME SORT OF "RAT MATH" IF YOU SEE ONE YOU'VE GOT 100! THEY ARE VERY DANGEROUS AND UNHEALTHY.
BUT PEASE KEEP IN MIND THE CHAIN REACTION IF YOUR NOT BEING RESPONSIBLE OR ATTENTIVE.
BEST OF LUCK AT $8.00 BUCKS A PIECE. INDOORS THEY WOULD WORK FINE. WATCH OUT IF YOU HAVE KIDS OR PETS.
CEW

top_screaming.text: USE A POINTED SPIKE POSSIBLY A 7/8. DRIVE THE SPIKE IN 18 INCHES. REMOVE SPIKE THEN THREAD ON ROD. THEN DRIVE IT IN THE SPIKE HOLE. YOU CAN EVEN USE A HAMMER DRILL FIRST WITH SAME SIZE BIT OR ELECTRIC JACKHAMMER FOR HARD ROCKY SOIL. PRE CREATING THE HOLE MAKES FOR EASY INSTALL. YOU MAY ONLY NEED A 3/4 SPIKE. MEASURE THE DIAMETER SIZE OF THE ANCHOR TO GET A SNUG FIT INTO THE HOLE. THEN WRAP THE INSTALL ROD AROUND CABLE OR THROUGH THE CABLE END AND SNATCH AND PULL UP TO LOCK. THEY MAKE DIFFERENT TYPES OF ANCHOR KITS. BUT IF YOU GET THESE IN PROPERLY THEY WILL HOLD ONCE ANCHOR HAS WEDGED IN HOLE SIDEWAYS. I HAVE USED THESE BEFORE. I WATCHED YOUTUBE AND FOUND EASIER METHODS TO GET ANCHORS INSERTED AND LOCKED INTO THE GROUND DEPENDING ON THE MATERIAL YOU HAVE UNDER THE SURFACE. AT FIRST I THOUGHT IT WAS GRASS AND DIRT UNTIL I FOUND OUT THE AMOUNT OF ROCKS IN THE GROUND IN WESTERN, MD. THAT BECAME A WHOLE NEW BALL GAME. AND MOST LIKELY THE INSTRUCTIONS WON'T BE SPECIFIC ENOUGH AS TO WHAT MATERIAL YOU ARE DEALING WITH UNDER THE GRASS SURFACE. ITS REALLY NOT THAT COMPLICATED. I MEASURED THE DIAMETER OF THE HOLE NEEDED TO GET THE ANCHOR INTO THE GROUND. DRIVE IT IN18 INCHES. THEN EITHER YOU CAN MIX UP SOME MORTOR OR CONCRETE AND PACK IT INTO THE HOLE AROUND THE CABLE AND JUST FILL UP TO SURFACE. FOR AN ADDED EXTRA. JUST DETERMINE WHETHER A SPIKE, HAMMER DRILL OR JACK HAMMER IS THE PROPER TOOL. ONCE INSTALLED YOU CAN DIG A SMALL OPENING BELOW THE SURFACE AROUND THE CABLE APPROX 6 IN DIAMETER. AND TAKE THE CABLE AND WRAP IT AND TUCK IT IN THE DEPRESSION. I CUT ROUND COVERS TO PRESS IN THE DEPRESSION TO BE ABLE TO MOW OVER THE CABLES. THEN JUS REMOVE THE WOOD PLUG AND UNWIND THE CABLE AND RECONNECT. I USE TURN BUCKLES WITH CLIPS TO ATTACH TO THE CANOPY FRAME. THEN USE THE TURN BUCKLES TO TIGHTEN THE CABLES. JUST NEED TO ADD A COUPLE OF EXTRAS TO MAKE THE JOB RIGHT AND TIGHT. NO ROCKET SCIENCE NEEDED. MY OLD CANOPIES I HAD SURVIVED HURRICANE ISABEL WITH 50 MPH WINDS BLOWING UP THE BAY AND THEY HELD PERFECT. I GOT 6 YRS ON THE CANOPIES THEN REPLACED. JUST WASH WITH CARWASH OR DISH LIQUID IN A BUCKET AND WASHBRUSH. YOU CAN SPRAY IT WITH SILICONE PERIOIDICALLY, OR I WAS GOING TO TRY THOMPSONS CLEAR WATER SEAL TO TRY AND ADD MORE TIME. BUT JUST CLEANING WHEN IT SHOWS TREE SAP OR DIRT JUST CLEAN IT WITH A WASHBRUSH, SOAP AND BUCKET. AND I ALWAYS CHECKE THE CABLES AND ROPES SECURE TO THE CANOPY. IF YOU USE NYLON 3/8 DOCK LINES THEY NEED TO BE TIGHTENED PERIODICALLY. THEY AREN'T A SET IT AND FORGET IT. BUT NOT A PITA.

VERY NICE CHAT WITH HER AND SHE EXPLAINED THE BRASS NOZZLE WAS SIMILAR TO THE NOZZLE ALREADY ON THE SPRAY WAND, AND DID THE SAME THING. BIG SHOUT OUT FOR ELAINE. THE SPRAYER PUMPED UP TO PRESSURE AFTER 15 UP AND DOWNS, AND AFTER ADJUSTING THE PLASTIC SPRAYER NOZZLE TO A SMALLER DIAMETER, I WAS IN BUSINESS.  THE ONLY MINUS IS THE CARRYING STRAP. ANOTHER NEAT GIZMO IS THE BLACK PLASTIC TRAY THE SPRAYER RESTS ON.  THE TRAY HAS A CUTOUT FOR THE SPRAYER HOSE WHEN IT IS CLIPPED TO THE SIDE OF THE SPRAYER AND CATCHES ANY SPRAY MATERIAL THAT MAY LEAK OUT.  SORT OF LIKE A DRIP PAN UNDER YOUR CAR. JUST BE SURE TO EMPTY THE CONTENTS OF THE SPRAYER AFTER USING AND RINSE OUT WITH WATER.  HANG IT UPSIDE DOWN TO EMPTY, AND YOU'LL BE GOOD TO GO FOR THE NEXT USE.  OH, ALMOST FORGOT- ALWAYS USE RUBBER GLOVES WHEN USING ANY YARD SPRAYER, AND WASH YOUR HANDS AFTER.  HOPE THIS HELPS.  THIS IS REALLY A GREAT PRODUCT.

top_exclaim.text:
I just got this yesterday and it's going back. There are other reviews like mine that are similar. I should paid attention to them, hopefully you will. I give it one star, but it doesn't deserve any. The item came, I put it together fine, plugged the charger in, put the battery in the charger....and "boink", the charger won't charge. I tried all advised troubleshooting. Nothing worked at all. I called their customer service. They seemed to agree that it was the charger and they would send a new one. Okay.....except the best they could do is get it here in 7 to 10 days. Now it is snowing like Hell outside and that just isn't going to work for me. I even told them I was going to return it, but that is the best they could do. 7 to 10 days from now!!!!!????? Come on', man!!!!! My final rating is: DON'T BUY IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Part 2:

I gave this a second chance and went on Amazon and bought a charger on my own instead of waiting on the company. It came two days later, this one worked and sure enough it charged the battery fine. I put it in the blower, pushed the power button, pulled back the impeller control and it worked. I was happpy.................for five minutes. I took it out outside, pushed the power button, pulled back the impeller blade and nothing happened. Oh yeah, the flashlight worked fine. I pulled out the battery and pushed it back in and pulled the impeller blade control and began to work on my drive way. The discharge worked okay. Once I worked one pass, I let go of the control bar, turned it around, pulled the bar back again.... and nothing. Pushed the power button on and off, pulled the blade....nothing. I pulled out the battery twice, reseated it, pushed the power putton, pulled the bar back and it ran again. Each time though that I let go of the impeller control bar, it would not restart. After, time after time of unsuccessfully restarting and then successfully starting employing the method I described, I did a few passes and then gave up. I was stupid for giving it a second chance. Now I am out the money for the charger that did not work to begin with and I am finally going to do what I said and return this piece of chunk. I said it before and I say it again, DON'T BUY THIS!!!!. I have never enjoyed packing up and returning something more in my life!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
top_exclaim.title: DON'T BUY THIS PIECE OF JUNK!!!!!!  YOU WILL BE SORRY!!!!!!!
