top_exclaim.text: oh boy! First of all this chair is MOST comfortable!! It arrived quickly/prime shipping....As the the 4th weekend fell upon us, I was happily unboxing and putting this out on the back deck! I opened the chair,reclined and thought I was on a cloud! So very happy and comfy! Then aprox. 7 minutes into relaxing I heard a single "click" from one of the locks...I thought nothing of it-perhaps I didn't lock all the way... I checked and it was locked... So back to enjoying the 4th weekend.....as  I was about to doze off under the summer sky....... click/click/click/click/click/click in rapid seccessions and the next thing I knew I was upside down feet over my head, my head crashed onto the deck.Now I am wedged between this chair and deck railing!!!! Not a great way to start off the weekend!!!! The description says "up to 300lbs" I am 235lbs!!! So I figured "I" made some sort of mistake or something...So I up righted the chair inspected,unlocked the locks had to forcefully push shut to start over.....So carefully I sit down and slowly I lean back and lock the locks....Now the weekend is better....for 4 minutes!!!! CRASH!!!!! same thing happened to me again!!!!!! NOT MY WEEKEND!!!! so then I decide "perhaps I somehow received a defective chair"...... So I go and get the other chair....I BOUGHT 2..... Carefully inspect,test with no one in it,slowly sit down and slowly lean back and lock the locks......ahhh....now this is what the weekend is all about!! nice comfy relaxing,and BAM!!!!!!! on the ground again!!!!!!!!!!!!.......3 times this chair and 2 time first chair!!!!!! all in 20 minutes!!!!!!!! VERY CHEAPLY MADE LOCKS!!!!!! DO NOT BUY IF YOU WEIGH OVER 150lbs!!!!!!!!!!! JUNK!!!!!!!!  I would return/but we burned the boxes to start the 4th of July bonfire......besides this had 4,093 reviews at 4.8 stars....what could possibly go wrong!?!?!? read ALL THE OTHER 1 star reviews....SAME THING HAPPENED TO THEM!!!!!! if you are a small person I am sure this would "work" ok for you, But I can not give this product any more than 1 star due to the description stating 300lb rating....CHEAP LOCKS!

Comparable to Combat. I used this in my office and only had to put two down before all evidence of mice stopped. There were feces and urine (since the bastards poo and pee on everything they walk on) all over the traps so I could see they were finding the bait. I didn't see anything 'fresh' for awhile so I just used a basic rag (no cleaning supplies so they couldn't smell chemicals) to wipe up around the baits, and I didn't see any more evidence after that. I haven't found any more chewed items (paper!!! JERKS!!!! CLOTHES!!! JERKS!!!) since then. I'm hoping the little darlings ate the bait to their hearts' content and will never be back. It was about three weeks from placing the bait to cessation of mouse activity so I'm thinking this got mom, dad, and all their bastard babies. YAY!
We live by a 40 acre field, I have food storage, and BIRDS (read: seed, pellets, etc) and these factors combined to cause us to have one hell of an infestation in our basement and in my office. (I even found a BABY SNAKE in my b asement!!!!! WTF!! Going where the food is???) I used Combat in the basement (all over) but still had infestation in my upstairs office. ONLY that room. No idea why as there is no food source in there but I'd run out of Combat when the Tomcat bait came up in Vine. I thought what a better time to test it than right now??? And work, it did! I hope I never have to use mouse bait again but if I do, Tomcat is coming home to mama!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
In the gooseneck arm/LED strip growlight category, this is one of the better ones. Anything less in terms of watts and the lights need to be inches from plants. You want 50-75 "watts" per arm, like these. I have 2.

Like all of these cheap LEDs, the timer and power switch is crap. Not compatible with timer plugs, must be clicked on manually. Unfortunately that's the case with literally every such cheap LED growlight I have.

The tripod is trash. OK yes it's somewhat functional but that's about it. Super flimsy. Takes up a huge amount of space. Luckily the light head takes a 1/4" camera mount, so you can use any standard tripod or clip. I immediately replaced the tripod with a clamp. Tripod is going in the trash.

I have natural light and big LED boards so these are just supplemental, and I wouldn't rely on them for a lot of light. But this kind of light is handy for optimizing light conditions, and it's good for more sensitive plants that can't take direct sunlight.

4/5, if you can get them on sale. -1 point for crappy/pointless tripod.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Decent for the category. Tripod is crap, replace it.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
My father purchased this generator/inverter for me as a present and he purchased it on Amazon. I received it yesterday, 2 days later than promised by Amazon Prime. When the box arrived I was shocked how large this generator was. Quite a bit bigger than the photos made it seem, but still smaller than my old Troybuilt Home Depot hunk of crap.

I was stunned to learn that this package did not come with oil. I understand shipping the generator with no oil in the motor, but for over $2k+, I would have expected 18oz of oil to have been in a container in the box; like other products come. Lucky I had some PRO Honda GN4 oil in my garage which I run in my atvs.

Next, I had to unbolt the front cover (4 - 10mm nuts) and connect the + and - wires to the provided battery. Took about 5 minutes. Pretty easy job. I followed the directions in the manual on page 88.

I hit the key and within just seconds the generator came alive. I cannot believe how quiet it is. At full speed, you can stand on top of the generator and have a normal conversation. You can pop the top to a soda or beer and hear the snap over this generator. I give it a top 5 start rating for how quiet it truly is. I have complete confidence that this generator will last a long time as all Honda products I own always live up to the readability test I put them through.

Although I read the product description and knew this generator weighed 130+ pounds, I did not realize how heavy 130+ pounds actually are. It is heavy. I used a dolly to move it. I could lift it on my own, but risk hurting myself unnecessarily.

I am only disappointed with two aspects of this purchase: 1 - how the hell did they not provide oil? I was lucky I had some oil on hand, but had I been in the field or in the woods not knowing this did not come with oil, I would have been pissed. 2- there is no hour meter which I knew when I decided I wanted this generator. But when the generator costs literally 3x more than Predator, I would have expected maybe throw in some oil and an hour meter. Knowing my disappointments are both very small and dumb to be upset about, I gave this generator 5 stars, because after all, it is awesome and worth the $2k+ that was paid for it.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Probably the best you can buy

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I don't have a bad fly problem because I bag and seal all of my trash, before I started using this death bag, I would see 5-10 flies congregating around the trash cans on a hot day. The problem is that my next-door neighbor doesn't bag and seal all their trash. Since our side yards are adjacent and the trash cans are about 5 feet apart the bag attracts all of their flies. I had hundreds if not a thousand flies in a week stuck in that bag, it is really disgusting to look at and on top of that I see at least 30-50 flies buzzing around the bag. It really smells like horrible roadkill. Do not put this close to a door or window particularly if it is upstream. Fortunately my trash cans are at the corner of the yard way from the house and windows and doors. I think the small is strong enough that it also attracts flies from within my immediate vicinity- probably another 2-4 houses.

I can see this being useful in isolated spots but for use in a typical suburban neighborhood you will attract way too many flies.

However if you don't bag and seal your trash and you have a bad fly problem this is perfect for you.

I can't really downgrade it because it works so well, I'm just not going to use them anymore.
BTW no difference from the one I bought at Home Depot, these are exactly the same.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Works too well, attracts flies from whole neigborhood

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I live about half an hour inland in NE Florida. Def hurricane impact territory.
Have the same typical complaints about the vagerity of the instructions and crappy wheels (REALLY CRAPPY!)
However, I have this genny in a specially (heavily) built shed out by the power pole on my property. It sits within a 4 post isolation frame with the bottom of the unit on a platform 2 feet off the ground. Makes changing the oil a breeze. In the construction of my shed I placed four treated lumber 4x4's (with a platform between) in concrete in the ground and basically built a 12x12' shed around it.
The shed is 80 feet from my house.
In the shed I have 6 100lb propane tanks.
My little 800sf house has never pulled more than 83 amps at once.
I have 2 5000btu window AC's and 1 8000btu unit. A standard over/under 22cf fridge, a small 16cf chest freezer, a washing machine, propane dryer (which only utilizes power to spin the drum and power the ignitor) and the standard assortment of kitchen appliances, radios, surround sounds, computers, TV's, etc.
I fired my genny up 5 different times over 2 days for a total of 5 hours of run time on (non-ethanol) (important for novice genny users!) gasoline. Changed the oil. Wasn't really dirty. Refilled the oil, integrating 1/4 a quart of Lucas oil treatment in this time.
Killed the power from the grid (via a master shut off at the pole)
Switched genny over to propane on one of the 100lb tanks. Fired it up. Had to play with the choke a little bit to get it to run smoothly on no load.
Threw switches to allow power to the house. All major appliances were powered off at their individual power supplies for my test.
Started with first the fridge and freezer. Generator didn't even sound like it cycled up. That's a good thing.
Next began adding window AC's. One 5000, two 5000, then the 8000. Definitely noticed the genny engine ramping up at 2nd AC power up.
Turned off all AC's except the 8000. Walked around the house turning on the most commonly used household items, everything worked fine. The generator is def running at higher RPMS.
I left the system running (off grid) for 46 hours nonstop before the first propane tank ran dry.
So I figure I have roughly 11 days of generator capacity in NE Florida during the hurricane season if I use my entire house power conservatively (Leave on only biggest AC in most common living area)
I've lived here 3 and a half years and experienced Matthew and Irma. (Irma screwed us!!)
Great peace of mind.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Great peace of mind

top_five_star_obscene.text:

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Wow! Ribs never tasted so good. Foolish of me to think I could use a regular grill as a smoker by using the divide the grill up in sections method. Silly of me to buy some piece of crap smoker for under $100. Lesson learned. I got tired of paying $20 for a rack of ribs at my local rib joint. Each time I did ribs, I got a little better, and a little better. What I was missing was a good smoker and the 3-2-1 method.

I made ribs using this unit for the first time this past weekend. And let me tell you, they were amazing! Be sure to Google the 3-2-1 method for smoking ribs. 3 hours, smoking, 2 hours wrapped and smoked, 1 hour unwrapped smoked and BBQ'd. Ribs come out just like I like them. Tender, smokey, but not "fall of the bone", these come out with a little bark, but still taste super tender and juicy!! I also smoked a whole chicken. Honestly, I wasn't the least bit impressed with the smoked chicken. Not sure why it just wasn't the same. Maybe it needed a wet sauce on it but I'll stick to deep frying my whole chickens.

Setup was breeze. Took under 30 minutes to setup. My unit has a small tear in the power cord, but I was hell bent on smoking ribs this weekend, I literally dreamed of good ribs all week, so even a tear in the cord wasn't going to stop me. It was negligible anyway. Setup requires simply attaching the handle, inserting racks, attaching plastic feed things, and installing trays.

The unit has a digital temperature LCD on front top to set the temp, and time. You can set a time and the smoker will turn off automatically at that time. Unfortunately this unit doesn't have a remote control. I was able to fit two racks of spare ribs, as well as a whole chicken.

The water tray is large, the wood try which was well designed slides out of the side, so you're not forced to open the door losing smoke and waiting for your temperature to restabilize. You simply slide it out, insert wood chips, then slide it back in, and rotate to dump the chips into the internal tray.

The door has a great seal and even latches closed to ensure tight seal. I never saw smoke escaping from around the door like the cheap crappy smoker I bought, that I used once and never touched it again.

The unit had no problem maintaining temperature which is one reason I knew I'd like the electric smoker, and maintain temperature it did.

This unit was well worth the money, would I purchase again? Absolutely!
top_five_star_obscene.title: Top Notch!  Killer Ribs!

Along with some cedar and pine branches that need removing to keep them out of the way. Well this little 9.0 amp saw did the trick. I was impressed with the motor and it's strength. I'm not felling trees, just loping off branches and cutting some old pine post. That being said elm and cedar are not the easiest woods to cut. I'm no spring chicken and when I went to use this saw even after I had assembled the bar and chain I was pleasantly surprised with the weight and ease it was to maneuver. Like other posters have mentioned this little saw is far better than the manual option. Keep an eye on the bar oil level it does use it up. I didn't have issues with the bar oil seeping or being thrown out in excess like others have mentioned. Now these next items might not mean much to some but now I can just put this saw in the shed and not worry about maintenance, maybe not to be used again for months. And, and, and your kids, relatives, neighbors won't ask you if they can borrow it some weekend to cut firewood. Well I have a neighbor that would ask, along with my generator and a couple of cords.
